blocks:
Sūrat al-Aḥzāb

(Medinan, and it consists of seventy-three verses by consensus) بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ اتَّقِ اللهَ وَلا تُطِعِ الْكافِرِينَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ (1) O Prophet! Be wary of Allah, and do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites.

In al-Qummi: This is what al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Allah sent His Prophet with [the principle of] 'I address you, but listen, O neighbor (إيّاك أعني واسمعي يا جارة).' Thus, the address is directed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the meaning is intended for the people."

In al-Majma': It was revealed concerning Abu Sufyan ibn Harb, 'Ikrimah ibn Abi Jahl, and Abu al-A'war al-Sulami. They came to Medina and stayed with 'Abd Allah ibn Ubayy after the Battle of Uhud, having been granted safe conduct (أمان) by the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم so that they might speak with him. So they stood up, and 'Abd Allah ibn Ubayy, 'Abd Allah ibn Sa'd ibn Abi Sarh, and Tu'mah ibn Abi Bayraq stood up with them. They entered upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, abandon the mention of our deities, al-Lat, al-'Uzza, and Manat, and say that they possess intercession (شفاعة) for whoever worships them, and we will leave you and your Lord." This distressed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. 'Umar ibn al-Khattab said: "Grant us permission, O Messenger of Allah, to kill them." He replied: "Indeed, I have granted them safe conduct (الأمان)." And he commanded (ص) that they be expelled from Medina. Then the verse was revealed.

وَلا تُطِعِ الْكافِرِينَ (and do not obey the faithless) refers to the people of Mecca: Abu Sufyan, Abu al-A'war, and 'Ikrimah; and وَالْمُنافِقِينَ (and the hypocrites) refers to Ibn Ubayy, Ibn Sa'd, and Tu'mah. إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلِيماً (Indeed, Allah is all-knowing) of the benefits (المصالح) and the harms (المفاسد), حَكِيماً (all-wise), He does not judge except with what wisdom (الحكمة) necessitates.

(٢) وَاتَّبِعْ ما يُوحى إِلَيْكَ مِنْ رَبِّكَ إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيراً ** (2) And follow what is revealed to you from your Lord. Indeed Allah is well aware of what you do. And it was recited with the yā’ (بالياء). (٣) وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ وَكَفى بِاللهِ وَكِيلاً (3) And put your trust in Allah, and Allah is sufficient as a trustee. (٤) ما جَعَلَ اللهُ لِرَجُلٍ مِنْ قَلْبَيْنِ فِي جَوْفِهِ ** (4) Allah has not put two hearts inside any man. His not gathering two hearts in one chest is a refutation of what the Arabs claimed: that the intelligent, discerning man (اللّبيب الأريب) has two hearts.

In al-Majma': It was revealed concerning Abu Ma'mar Humayd ibn Ma'mar ibn Habib al-Fihri, who was intelligent and retained what he heard. He used to say, "Indeed, I have two hearts in my chest, and with each one, I reason better than Muhammad's intellect (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم)." Quraysh used to call him Dhū al-Qalbayn (the one with two hearts). When the Day of Badr arrived and the polytheists were defeated, among them was Abu Ma'mar. Abu Sufyan ibn Harb met him while he was holding one of his sandals in his hand and the other was on his foot. Abu Sufyan said to him, "O Abu Ma'mar, what is the state of the people?" He replied, "They have been defeated." Abu Sufyan asked, "Then what is the matter with you, that one of your sandals is in your hand and the other is on your foot?" Abu Ma'mar replied, "I did not realize except that both of them were on my feet." So, they knew that day that he had only one heart, because he forgot his sandal in his hand.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), who said: The Commander of the Faithful, Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), said: "The love for us and the love for our enemy cannot gather in the chest of a human being. Indeed, Allah has not put two hearts inside any man **قَلْبَيْنِ فِي جَوْفِهِ ** so that he loves with one and hates with the other. As for the one who loves us, his love for us is purified, just as gold is purified by fire, with no impurity in it. So, whoever wishes to know his love for us, let him test his heart. If the love for our enemy shares in his love for us, then he is not from us, and we are not from him. And Allah is their enemy, and Jibril and Mika'il, and Allah is an enemy to the disbelievers."

And in al-Amali: what is close to this meaning.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah has not put two hearts inside any man **ما جَعَلَ اللهُ لِرَجُلٍ مِنْ قَلْبَيْنِ ** so that he loves a people with one and loves their enemies with the other.

And in Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Whoever's heart is attached during his prayer to something other than Allah, he is close to that thing and far from the reality of what Allah intended from him in his prayer." Then He recited this Ayah: nor has He made your wives whom you declare ẓihār to be your mothers. And it was recited with the yā’ alone, without the hamzah (بدون همزة), whom you declare ẓihār to be your mothers تُظاهِرُونَ مِنْهُنَ . And it was recited with the ḍammah on the tā’ (بضمّ التاء), and the shaddah on the ẓā’ (وتشديد الظّاء), and by omitting the alif and placing the shaddah on the ẓā’ and the hā’ (وبحذف الالف وتشديد الظاء والهاء). (1)

And His not gathering wifehood (الزوجيّة) and motherhood (الأمومة) in one woman is a refutation of what the Arabs claimed: that whoever said to his wife, "You are to me like the back of my mother," his wife became like a mother to him. The full discussion on this will come in Surat al-Mujadalah, if Allah wills.

وَما جَعَلَ أَدْعِياءَكُمْ أَبْناءَكُمْ ** nor has He made your adopted sons your [real] sons. And His not gathering adoption (الدّعوة) and sonship (البنوّة) in a man is a refutation of what the Arabs claimed: that a man's adopted son (دعي الرّجل) was his son. For this reason, they used to call Zayd ibn Harithah al-Kalbi, the freed slave of the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), "the son of Muhammad" (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم).

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: The reason for this was that when the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) married Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, He went out to the market of Ukaz (عكاظ) for a trade of hers. He saw Zayd being sold and saw that he was a clever, discerning youth, so He bought him. When the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was commissioned as a Prophet (نُبِّىءَ), He invited him to Islam, and he accepted Islam. He was called Zayd, the freed slave (مولى) of Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). When the news of his son Zayd reached Harithah ibn Sharahil al-Kalbi, he came to Mecca. He was a noble man, so he went to Abu Talib and said, "O Abu Talib, my son was taken captive, and I have heard that he is with your nephew. Ask him either to sell him, or to ransom him, or to free him." So, Abu Talib spoke to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "He is free; let him go wherever He wishes." Harithah stood up and took Zayd by the hand and said to him, "O my son, join your honor and your lineage." Zayd said, "I will never leave the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم)." His father said to him, "Will you abandon your lineage and ancestry and be a slave to Quraysh?" Zayd said, "I will not leave the Messenger of Allah as long as I live."

His father became angry and said, "O assembly of Quraysh, bear witness that I am free of him, and he is not my son!" The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "Bear witness that Zayd is my son; I inherit from him, and he inherits from me." So, he was called Zayd ibn Muhammad. The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) loved him and named him Zayd al-Ḥubb (Zayd the Beloved). When the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) migrated to Medina, He married him to Zaynab bint Jaḥsh. One day, Zayd was late, so the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) came to his house to ask about him. He found Zaynab sitting in the middle of her room, grinding perfume with a pestle (فهر) of hers. The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) pushed the door open and looked at her. She was beautiful and lovely. He said: "Glory be to Allah, the Creator of Light! So blessed is Allah, the best of creators! فَتَبارَكَ اللهُ أَحْسَنُ الْخالِقِينَ " Then the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) returned to His house, and Zaynab made a strange impression upon His heart. Zayd came to his house, and Zaynab informed him of what the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) had said. Zayd said to her, "Would you like me to divorce you so that the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) may marry you? Perhaps you have made an impression upon His heart." She said, "I fear that if you divorce me, the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) will not marry me." Zayd came to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and said, "May my father and mother be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah! Zaynab informed me of such and such. Would you like me to divorce her so that You may marry her?" The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said to him: "No, go and fear Allah, and Hold on to your wife أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ ." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, related this, saying: Hold on to your wife, and be wary of Allah. You were concealing in your heart what Allah was going to disclose, and you were fearing the people, while Allah is worthier of being feared. So when Zayd had accomplished his purpose with her, We married her to you, up to His saying: and Allah’s command is bound to be fulfilled. وَكانَ أَمْرُ اللهِ مَفْعُولاً ** So, Allah the Almighty married Him to her from above His Throne. The hypocrites said, "He forbids us the wives of our sons, yet He marries the wife of his son Zayd." So, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed concerning this: nor has He made your adopted sons your [real] sons وَما جَعَلَ أَدْعِياءَكُمْ أَبْناءَكُمْ ** up to His saying: He guides to the way. يَهْدِي السَّبِيلَ.

I say: The story of the marriage of Zaynab to the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) will come in another form in this Surah, if Allah wills. That is [only] a remark of your mouths, ذلِكُمْ قَوْلُكُمْ بِأَفْواهِكُمْ ** which has no reality, like the saying of one who raves. But Allah speaks the truth, وَاللهُ يَقُولُ الْحَقَ ** which belongs to Him, and He guides to the way وَهُوَ يَهْدِي السَّبِيلَ —the way of truth (سبيل الحقّ).

(1) It is said: He declared ẓihār (ظاهر) from his wife, or taẓāhara (تظاهر), or taẓahhara (تظهر): which is when he says to her, "You are to me like the back of my mother." The Arabs used to divorce their wives in the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) with this phrase, but when Islam came, it forbade it and made expiation (الكفّارة) obligatory for it.

(٥) ادْعُوهُمْ لِآبائِهِمْ ** (5) Call them after their fathers; Refer them (انسبوهم) to them (their fathers). هُوَ أَقْسَطُ عِنْدَ اللهِ ** that is more just with Allah. It means more just (أعدل). What is intended by it is absolute increase (مطلق الزيادة), not preference (التفضيل), and its meaning is the utmost degree (البالغ) in truthfulness (الصدق). فَإِنْ لَمْ تَعْلَمُوا آباءَهُمْ ** But if you do not know their fathers, so that you may refer them (لتنسبوهم) to them (the fathers). فَإِخْوانُكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ then they are your brethren in faith then they are your brethren in faith (فهم إخوانكم في الدين) and your allies (ومواليكم) and your guardians (وأولياؤكم) therein. So they may say: "This is my brother and my ally (مولاي)" by this interpretation (التّأويل). وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ فِيما أَخْطَأْتُمْ بِهِ ** There is no sin upon you for what you commit by mistake, And there is no sin (ولا اثم) upon you for what you did of that by mistake (مخطئين), whether before the prohibition (النهي) or after it due to forgetfulness (النسيان) or a slip of the tongue (سبق اللّسان). وَلكِنْ ما تَعَمَّدَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً ** but [you are accountable] for what your hearts deliberately intend. And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. He pardons (يعفو) the one who errs (المخطي).

(٦) النَّبِيُّ أَوْلى بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ (6) The Prophet is closer to the believers than their own souls meaning, He is closer to them in all affairs, for He does not command them nor is He pleased with them except with that which contains their rectification (صلاحهم) and success (نجاحهم), contrary to the soul (النفس). Therefore, this was stated absolutely, and it is obligatory upon them that He be more beloved to them than their own souls, and His command be more effective upon them than the command of their souls, and their compassion for Him be more complete than their compassion for their own souls. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: When He intended the Battle of Tabuk and commanded the people to go forth, some people said, "We must ask permission from our fathers and mothers." So this verse was revealed. And narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq عليهما السلام: They both recited: وَأَزْواجُهُ أُمَّهاتُهُمْ (and his wives are their mothers) وهو أبٌ لهم (and He is a father to them). And al-Qummi said: It was revealed وهو أب لهم (and He is a father to them). I say: meaning, in religion (الدين) and in the world (الدنيا) both. As for religion, every Prophet is a father to his nation (أمّته) because He is the origin of that through which eternal life is attained. For this reason, the believers became brothers. It is also narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He said: "Ali and I are the two fathers of this nation," as mentioned in Surat al-Baqarah. This is because they are equal in this meaning, except that Ali is after the Prophet. As for the world, it is due to Allah obligating Him to bear their maintenance (مؤنتهم), the upbringing of their orphans (أيتامهم), and those among them who are neglected (يضيع منهم). Al-Qummi said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made the believers the children of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and made the Messenger of Allah their father for those who were unable to protect themselves, had no wealth, and had no authority (ولاية) over their own souls. Thus, Allah the Almighty granted His Prophet the authority (الولاية) over the believers and made Him closer to the believers than their own souls. This is the saying of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم at Ghadir Khumm: "O people, am I not closer to you than your own souls?" They said: "Yes, indeed." Then He obligated for the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام the same authority (الولاية) over them that He had obligated for Himself, saying: "Behold, whoever I am his Mawla (مولاه), Ali is his Mawla (مولاه)." When Allah made the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم a father to the believers, He obligated Him to bear their maintenance and the upbringing of their orphans. At that point, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ascended the pulpit (المنبر) and said: "Whoever leaves behind wealth, it is for his heirs; and whoever leaves behind a debt or dependents (ضياعاً), it is upon me and to me (فعليَّ وإليَ)." Thus, Allah obligated His Prophet towards the believers with what is obligatory upon a parent towards a child, and obligated the believers with obedience to Him with what is obligatory upon a child towards a parent. Likewise, He obligated the Commander of the Faithful with what He obligated the Messenger of Allah thereafter, and after Him, the Imams, one by one. He said: And the proof that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام are the two parents (والدان) is His saying: وَاعْبُدُوا اللهَ وَلا تُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئاً وَبِالْوالِدَيْنِ إِحْساناً (36) and worship Allah, and do not ascribe any partners to Him, and be good to the parents So the two parents (الوالدان) are the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. And al-Sadiq عليه السلام said: The conversion to Islam of the generality of the Jews was due to this reason, because they felt secure regarding themselves and their families (عيالاتهم). In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: He was asked why the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was given the kunya (كنّي) of Abu al-Qasim (أبي القاسم). He replied: "Because He had a son named al-Qasim, and He was given the kunya after him." The questioner said: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, do you see me worthy of an addition?" He replied: "Yes. Did you not know that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'Ali and I are the two fathers of this nation'?" He said: "Yes, indeed." He said: "Did you not know that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is a father to His entire nation, and Ali is one of them?" He said: "Yes, indeed." He said: "Did you not know that Ali عليه السلام is the distributor (قاسم) of Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار)?" He said: "Yes, indeed." He said: "So He was called Abu al-Qasim because He is the father of the distributor (قاسم) of Paradise and the Fire." He said: "Yes, indeed." He said: "And what is the meaning of that?" He replied: "The compassion (شفقة) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم for His nation is like the compassion of fathers for their children. And the best of His nation is Ali عليه السلام, and after Him, the compassion of Ali عليه السلام for them is like His compassion, because He is His successor (وصيّه), His vicegerent (خليفته), and the Imam after Him. For this reason, He said: 'Ali and I are the two fathers of this nation.' And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ascended the pulpit (المنبر) and said: 'Whoever leaves behind a debt or dependents (ضياعاً), it is upon me and to me; and whoever leaves behind wealth, it is for his heirs.' Thus, by this, He became closer than their fathers and mothers, and He became closer to them than their own souls. Likewise, the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام after Him—that occurred for him just as it occurred for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." In al-Kafi, narrated from Sulaym ibn Qays, who said: I heard Abdullah ibn Ja'far al-Tayyar saying: I, al-Hasan and al-Husayn عليهما السلام, Abdullah ibn Abbas, Umar ibn Umm Salamah, and Usamah ibn Zayd were with Mu'awiyah. A discussion took place between me and Mu'awiyah, so I said to Mu'awiyah: I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم saying: "I am closer to the believers than their own souls; then my brother Ali ibn Abi Talib is closer to the believers than their own souls; and when he is martyred, then al-Hasan ibn Ali is closer to the believers than their own souls; then my son al-Husayn after him is closer to the believers than their own souls; and when he is martyred, then his son Ali ibn al-Husayn is closer to the believers than their own souls—and you will reach him, O Ali—then his son Muhammad ibn Ali is closer to the believers than their own souls—and you will reach him, O Husayn—then the completion of the twelve Imams, nine of whom are from the progeny of al-Husayn عليهم السلام." Abdullah ibn Ja'far said: And I called upon al-Hasan, al-Husayn, Abdullah ibn Abbas, Umar ibn Umm Salamah, and Usamah ibn Zayd to bear witness, and they testified for me before Mu'awiyah. Sulaym said: And I had heard that from Salman, Abu Dharr, and al-Miqdad, and they mentioned that they heard that from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I am closer to every believer than his own soul, and Ali is closer to him after me." He was asked: "What is the meaning of that?" He replied: "The saying of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: 'Whoever leaves behind a debt or dependents (ضياعاً), it is upon me and to me; and whoever leaves behind wealth, it is for his heirs.' A man has no authority (ولاية) over his own soul if he has no wealth, and he has no command or prohibition over his family (عياله) if he does not provide maintenance (النفقة) for them. The Prophet and the Commander of the Faithful, and those after them, peace of Allah be upon them, were obligated to this. From there, they became closer to them than their own souls. And the reason for the conversion to Islam of the generality of the Jews was only after this saying from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and that they felt secure regarding themselves and their families (عيالاتهم)." In Nahj al-Balaghah, in a narration of his, he said: "By Allah, I am indeed the closest of people to the people." and his wives are their mothers—they are placed in His position regarding absolute prohibition (of marriage) and regarding the deserving of reverence, as long as they remain obedient to Allah. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام in a narration: And the wives of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم are like their mothers in sanctity (الحرمة). In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Qa'im عليه السلام: He was asked about the meaning of the divorce (الطلاق) whose ruling the Messenger of Allah entrusted to the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. He said: "Indeed, Allah, sanctified is His Name, magnified the status of the wives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and singled them out with the honor of being mothers. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'O Abu al-Hasan, this honor remains as long as they remain obedient. Whichever of them disobeys Allah after me by rising against you, then divorce her from the wives and drop her from the honor of being mothers and from the honor of the motherhood of the believers.'" وَأُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ فِي كِتابِ اللهِ (6) But the blood relatives are closer to one another in Allah’s Book in His written decree (حكمه المكتوب). Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Imamate (الإمامة). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He was asked concerning this verse, regarding whom it was revealed. He said: "It was revealed concerning the leadership (الامرة). Indeed, this verse applies to the progeny of al-Husayn عليه السلام after him. So we are closer to the authority (الأمر) and to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم than the Emigrants (المهاجرين) and the Helpers (الأنصار)." I say: This exact verse has already passed at the end of Surat al-Anfal, and that it was revealed to abrogate inheritance based on emigration (الهجرة) and support (النصرة). Reconciling the revelation of this verse concerning leadership (الامرة) and that verse concerning inheritance (الميراث) does not suit the exception in this verse, nor what follows in its explanation, unless it is said that the leadership (الامرة) is an esoteric interpretation (تأويل), as is understood from what follows, narrated from al-Ilal regarding His Almighty saying: أَنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ (the verse). And by generalizing the meaning in both verses, the contradiction is removed. مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُهاجِرِينَ (from the believers and the Emigrants) is connected to "blood relatives" (أولي الأرحام), meaning: the blood relatives by right of kinship (القرابة) are closer to the leadership (الامرة) or to the inheritance (الميراث) than the believers by right of religion (الدين) and the Emigrants by right of emigration (الهجرة). And if we interpret the verse concerning inheritance, it is also possible that it is an explanation for the blood relatives: إِلَّا أَنْ تَفْعَلُوا إِلى أَوْلِيائِكُمْ مَعْرُوفاً (6) except that you do some good to your friends meaning by it, the bequest (الوصيّة). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: He was asked what share the clients (الموالي) have. He replied: "They have nothing of the inheritance except what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: إِلَّا أَنْ تَفْعَلُوا إِلى أَوْلِيائِكُمْ مَعْرُوفاً. كانَ ذلِكَ فِي الْكِتابِ مَسْطُوراً (6) That has been inscribed in the Book meaning, what was mentioned in the two verses is established in the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح). Thus it was said.

(٧) وَإِذْ أَخَذْنا ** [is] understood [in the context of] 'Mention' مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِيثاقَهُمْ وَمِنْكَ وَمِنْ نُوحٍ وَإِبْراهِيمَ وَمُوسى وَعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ وَأَخَذْنا مِنْهُمْ مِيثاقاً غَلِيظاً ** (And when We took a covenant from the prophets, and from you, and from Noah, Abraham, Moses, and Jesus son of Mary, and We took from them a solemn covenant) Al-Qummi said: This waw (الواو) is an addition in His saying وَمِنْكَ ; rather, it is مِنْكَ وَمِنْ نُوحٍ . So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, took the covenant for Himself upon the prophets. Then He took [a covenant] for His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم upon the prophets and the Imams عليهم السلام. Then He took [a covenant] for the prophets upon His Messenger.

(٨) لِيَسْئَلَ الصَّادِقِينَ عَنْ صِدْقِهِمْ ** (8) that He may question the truthful about their truthfulness. We did that so that Allah may question, on the Day of Resurrection, the Prophets who fulfilled their covenant, and thus their truthfulness will become manifest. وَأَعَدَّ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَذاباً أَلِيماً and He has prepared for the faithless a painful punishment. It is as if it was said: So He rewarded the believers, وَأَعَدَّ لِلْكافِرِينَ** (and He has prepared for the faithless).

(٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ جاءَتْكُمْ جُنُودٌ ** (9) O you who have faith, remember Allah’s blessing upon you, when hosts came to you, meaning the Confederates (الأحزاب), who were Quraysh, Ghatafan, and the Jews of Qurayzah and al-Nadir. فَأَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحاً ** then We sent against them a wind, meaning the west wind (ريح الدبور). وَجُنُوداً لَمْ تَرَوْها ** and hosts that you did not see, meaning the angels (الملائكة). وَكانَ اللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيراً ** Allah is All-seeing of what you do, referring to the digging of the trench (حفر الخندق). And it was recited with the yā’ (بالياء), meaning: concerning the confederating (التحزّب) and the fighting (المحاربة).

(١٠) إِذْ جاؤُكُمْ مِنْ فَوْقِكُمْ ** (10) When they came at you from above you from above the valley وَمِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنْكُمْ ** and from below you from below the valley وَإِذْ زاغَتِ الْأَبْصارُ ** and when the eyes turned aside deviated from their level of sight due to confusion and staring (شخوصاً) وَبَلَغَتِ الْقُلُوبُ الْحَناجِرَ and the hearts reached the throats due to terror, for the lung (الريّة) swells from the intensity of the fright (الرّوع), and rises with its swelling to the top of the throat (الحنجرة), which is the end of the windpipe (الحلقوم) وَتَظُنُّونَ بِاللهِ الظُّنُونَا ** and you entertained misgivings about Allah. the various types of conjecture (الظنّ). And it was recited (قرئ) by omitting the alif (الألف) both when connecting (the recitation) and generally (مطلقاً).

(11) هُنالِكَ ابْتُلِيَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ** (There the faithful were tested) They were tested, so the sincere (المخلص) became distinct from the hypocrite (المنافق), and the steadfast (الثابت) from the wavering (المتزلزل). وَزُلْزِلُوا زِلْزالاً شَدِيداً ** (and were shaken with a mighty shaking.) due to the intensity of the terror. (12) وَإِذْ يَقُولُ الْمُنافِقُونَ وَالَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ ما وَعَدَنَا اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ ** (And when the hypocrites and those in whose hearts was a sickness said, 'Allah and His Apostle did not promise us) concerning victory and the exaltation of the religion إِلَّا غُرُوراً ** (anything but delusion.') a false promise. (13) وَإِذْ قالَتْ طائِفَةٌ مِنْهُمْ يا أَهْلَ يَثْرِبَ ** (And when a faction of them said, 'O people of Yathrib!) O people of the city (المدينة) لا مُقامَ لَكُمْ ** (There is no place for you to stay,) there is no place of standing for you here. And it was recited with a dammah on the mīm (مُقام) on the basis that it is a place (مكان) or a verbal noun (مصدر) derived from residence (الإقامة). فَارْجِعُوا ** (so go back.') to your dwellings, fleeing. وَيَسْتَأْذِنُ فَرِيقٌ مِنْهُمُ النَّبِيَ ** (And a group of them sought permission from the Prophet) for returning. يَقُولُونَ إِنَّ بُيُوتَنا عَوْرَةٌ ** (saying, 'Our houses are exposed.') They are not fortified (حصينة), and its origin (أصلها) is defect (الخلل). وَما هِيَ بِعَوْرَةٍ. (Yet they were not exposed.) In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Rather, they were high-roofed (رفيعة السّمك) and fortified (حصينة). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Their houses were on the outskirts of the dwellings where people were isolated, so He gave the lie to them, saying: وَما هِيَ بِعَوْرَةٍ (Yet they were not exposed.) إِنْ يُرِيدُونَ إِلَّا فِراراً ** (They only desire to flee) from fighting.

(14) وَلَوْ دُخِلَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ أَقْطارِها ** (Even if it were entered upon them from its quarters) from its sides (جوانبها) ثُمَّ سُئِلُوا الْفِتْنَةَ ** (and then they were asked to commit sedition) apostasy (الردّة) and fighting the Muslims لَآتَوْها ** (they would surely commit it). they would surely give it (أعطوها). And it was read with al-qasr (بالقصر). وَما تَلَبَّثُوا بِها ** (and they would not tarry in it) with the temptation (الفتنة), meaning: by giving it إِلَّا يَسِيراً. (except a little).

(١٥) وَلَقَدْ كانُوا عاهَدُوا اللهَ مِنْ قَبْلُ لا يُوَلُّونَ الْأَدْبارَ وَكانَ عَهْدُ اللهِ مَسْؤُلاً ** (15) And certainly they had pledged to Allah earlier that they would not turn their backs, and Allah’s pledge is bound to be questioned. (This refers to being questioned) concerning fulfilling it (الوفاء به). (١٦) قُلْ لَنْ يَنْفَعَكُمُ الْفِرارُ إِنْ فَرَرْتُمْ مِنَ الْمَوْتِ أَوِ الْقَتْلِ ** (16) Say: The flight will not benefit you if you flee from death or killing. For every person must inevitably meet a natural death (حتف انف) or be killed at a specific time which the Divine Decree (القضاء) has preceded and upon which the Pen (القلم) has run. وَإِذاً لا تُمَتَّعُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** and then you will not be given enjoyment except for a little while. Meaning: Even if the flight benefited you, for example, and you were granted enjoyment (فمتْعتم) through delay, that enjoyment would not be anything but a brief enjoyment (تمتيعاً) or a short period (زماناً قليلاً).

(١٧) قُلْ مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يَعْصِمُكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ إِنْ أَرادَ بِكُمْ سُوءاً أَوْ أَرادَ بِكُمْ رَحْمَةً وَلا يَجِدُونَ لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَلِيًّا ** (17) Say, 'Who is it that can protect you from Allah if He desires to cause you some harm, or desires to show you some mercy?' And they will not find for themselves any guardian besides Allah, nor any helper. — who benefits them — **وَلا نَصِيراً ** — nor any helper — — who repels harm from them.

(18) قَدْ يَعْلَمُ اللهُ الْمُعَوِّقِينَ مِنْكُمْ ** (18) Allah certainly knows those who hold back among you Those who discourage (المثبطين) from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and they are the hypocrites (المنافقون). وَالْقائِلِينَ لِإِخْوانِهِمْ هَلُمَّ إِلَيْنا ** (and those who say to their brethren, 'Come to us!') Bring yourselves near to us. **وَلا يَأْتُونَ الْبَأْسَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** (and they do not come to the battle except a little) And they do not fight except a little.

(19) أَشِحَّةً عَلَيْكُمْ ** (19) [They are] stingy towards you. It was said: [They are] miserly towards you regarding assistance (المعاونة), or spending (النفقة) in the way of Allah (في سبيل الله), or victory (الظفر) and spoils (الغنيمة). فَإِذا جاءَ الْخَوْفُ رَأَيْتَهُمْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ تَدُورُ أَعْيُنُهُمْ ** But when fear comes, you see them looking at you, their eyes rolling within their sockets (أحداقهم), like one who is overcome (يُغْشى عَلَيْهِ), like the gaze of one who is overcome, مِنَ الْمَوْتِ ** because of [the fear of] death. due to the struggle (معالجة) of the agonies of death (سكرات الموت), out of fear and seeking refuge (لواذاً) with you. فَإِذا ذَهَبَ الْخَوْفُ ** But when the fear is gone, and the spoils (الغنائم) are secured (حيّزت), سَلَقُوكُمْ ** they assault you they strike you (ضربوكم) بِأَلْسِنَةٍ حِدادٍ ** with sharp tongues, with sharp (ذربة) tongues, seeking the spoils (الغنيمة). And al-salq (السلق) means extending (البسط) and overpowering (القهر) by hand or by tongue. أَشِحَّةً عَلَى الْخَيْرِ أُولئِكَ لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا ** being stingy with the good. They have not believed, [They have not believed] with sincerity (اخلاصاً), فَأَحْبَطَ اللهُ أَعْمالَهُمْ وَكانَ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيراً so Allah has rendered their works void, and that is easy for Allah. and that was easy (يَسِيراً) for Allah, [meaning] light (هيّناً).

(٢٠) يَحْسَبُونَ الْأَحْزابَ لَمْ يَذْهَبُوا ** (They suppose the confederates have not gone away) meaning, these people, due to their cowardice, imagine that the confederates have not been defeated, though they have been defeated. وَإِنْ يَأْتِ الْأَحْزابُ ** (And should the confederates come) a second time, يَوَدُّوا لَوْ أَنَّهُمْ بادُونَ فِي الْأَعْرابِ ** (they would wish they were in the desert among the Bedouins) they would wish that they were outside in the desert (البدو) and settled among the Bedouins (الأعراب), يَسْئَلُونَ ** (inquiring) asking every newcomer from the direction of Medina عَنْ أَنْبائِكُمْ ** (about your news) concerning what has happened to you. وَلَوْ كانُوا فِيكُمْ ** (And if they were among you) this time, and had not returned to Medina, and there was fighting, ما قاتَلُوا إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** (they would not fight except a little) out of hypocrisy (رياء) and fear of reproach (التعيير).

Al-Qummi narrates: These verses were revealed concerning the story of the Confederates (الأحزاب) from Quraysh and the Arabs who formed a coalition against the Messenger of Allah. He said: That was because Quraysh gathered in the fifth year of the Hijra (الهجرة), traveled to the Arabs, rallied them, and incited them to fight the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. They arrived with ten thousand men, accompanied by Kinanah, Sulaym, and Fazārah.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله had previously expelled Banu al-Nadir, who were a clan of Jews from Medina. Their leader was Huyayy ibn Akhtab, and they were Jews from the lineage of Aaron (upon our Prophet and his family and upon him be peace). When He expelled them from Medina, they went to Khaybar. Huyayy ibn Akhtab went out to Quraysh in Mecca and said to them: "Muhammad has wronged you and wronged us. He expelled us from Medina, from our homes and our wealth, and He expelled our cousins, Banu Qaynuqa. So travel throughout the land and gather your allies and others until we march against them. For there remain seven hundred fighters of my people in Yathrib, who are Banu Qurayzah. There is a covenant and treaty between them and Muhammad, and I will persuade them to break the covenant between them and Muhammad, and they will be with us against them. You will come from above, and they will come from below." The location of Banu Qurayzah was about two miles from Medina, in the place called Bi’r Banī al-Muṭṭalib. Huyayy ibn Akhtab continued traveling with them among the Arab tribes until they gathered about ten thousand men from Quraysh and Kinanah, and Al-Aqra' ibn Habis with his people, and Abbas ibn Mirdas with Banu Sulaym. This news reached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, so He consulted His Companions, who numbered seven hundred men. Salman al-Fārisī (may Allah have mercy on him) said: "O Messenger of Allah, the few cannot withstand the many in a prolonged engagement." He asked: "What should we do?" He replied: "We should dig a trench (خندقاً) that will be a barrier between you and them, allowing you to prolong the engagement with them, and preventing them from reaching us from every direction. For we, the people of Persia (العجم) in the lands of Persia (فارس), when an enemy attack threatened us, we would dig trenches, so the fighting would occur only from known positions."

Then Gabriel descended upon the Messenger of Allah and said: "He has advised correctly." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded that it be marked out from the direction of Uhud to Rātij. He assigned a group of the Emigrants (المهاجرين) and the Helpers (الأنصار) to dig every twenty or thirty paces. The spades (المساحي) and picks (المعاول) were brought, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله began, taking a pick and digging Himself in the section assigned to the Emigrants. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was transporting the soil from the excavation until the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله sweated and became tired, and He said: "There is no life but the life of the Hereafter. O Allah, forgive the Helpers (الأنصار) and the Emigrants (المهاجرين)." When the people saw the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله digging, they exerted themselves in the excavation and transported the soil. On the second day, they started digging early, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله sat in the Mosque of the Conquest (مسجد الفتح). While the Emigrants and the Helpers were digging, a mountain appeared before them that the picks could not affect. They sent Jābir ibn Abd Allāh al-Ansārī to inform the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله about it. Jābir said: "I came to the Mosque, and the Messenger of Allah was lying on His back, with His cloak beneath His head, and He had tied a stone to His stomach. I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, a mountain has appeared before us that the picks cannot affect.' He stood up quickly and came to it. Then He called for water in a vessel, washed His face and forearms, and wiped His head and feet. Then He drank, gargled some of that water in His mouth, and then poured it onto that stone. Then He took a pick and struck a blow, and a flash of light shone, in which we saw the palaces of Syria (الشام). Then He struck another blow, and a flash of light shone, in which we saw the palaces of Al-Madā’in. Then He struck another blow, and another flash of light shone, in which we saw the palaces of Yemen (اليمن). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: 'Indeed, Allah will open for you these places where the flashes shone.' Then the mountain crumbled upon us like collapsing sand."

Jābir said: "I realized that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was hungry (مقوى), meaning starving, because I saw the stone on His stomach. I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, would You like some food?' He asked: 'What do you have, O Jābir?' I replied: 'A young goat (عناق) and a ṣāʿ (صاع) of barley.' He said: 'Go ahead and prepare what you have.'" Jābir said: "I went to my family and ordered her to grind the barley, slaughter the goat, skin it, and ordered her to bake, cook, and roast. When I finished that, I came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and said: 'May my father and mother be sacrificed for You, O Messenger of Allah, we have finished, so please come with whomever You wish.'" He stood up at the edge of the trench and then said: "O company of the Emigrants and the Helpers, answer Jābir's call!" Jābir said: "There were seven hundred men in the trench, and they all came out. He did not pass by any of the Emigrants or the Helpers except that He said: 'Answer Jābir's call!'" I went ahead and said to my family: "By Allah, Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, has come to you with something you cannot handle." She asked: "Did you inform Him of what we have?" I said: "Yes." She said: "Then He knows best what He has come for." Jābir said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله entered and looked into the pot (القدر), then said: 'Serve and leave some.' Then He looked into the oven (التنّور), then said: 'Take out and leave some.' Then He called for a large dish (صحفة), crumbled bread into it (ثرد), and served. He said: 'O Jābir, bring them in ten by ten.' So I brought in ten, and they ate until they were satisfied (نهلوا), and nothing was seen in the dish except the traces of their fingers. Then He said: 'O Jābir, bring me the foreleg (الذّراع).' So I brought Him the foreleg, and they ate it. Then He said: 'Bring in ten,' so I brought them in until they ate and were satisfied, and nothing was seen in the dish except the traces of their fingers. Then He said: 'O Jābir, bring me the foreleg.' So they ate and left. Then He said: 'Bring in ten,' so I brought them in, and they ate until they were satisfied, and nothing was seen in the dish except the traces of their fingers. Then He said: 'Bring me the foreleg.' So I brought it to Him. I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, how many forelegs does a sheep have?' He said: 'Two forelegs.' I said: 'By Him Who sent You with the truth, I brought You three!' He said: 'If only you had remained silent, O Jābir, all the people would have eaten from the foreleg.'" Jābir said: "I continued bringing them in ten by ten, and they ate until they had all eaten, and by Allah, enough of that food remained for us to live on for days."

He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله dug the trench and made eight gates for it, placing a man from the Emigrants and a man from the Helpers, along with a group, to guard each gate. Quraysh, Kinanah, Sulaym, and Hilāl arrived and camped at Al-Zaghābah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله finished digging the trench three days before the arrival of Quraysh. Quraysh approached, accompanied by Huyayy ibn Akhtab. When they camped at Al-ʿAqīq, Huyayy ibn Akhtab came to Banu Qurayzah in the middle of the night. They were inside their fortresses, holding fast to the covenant of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him).

He knocked on the gate of the fortress. Kaʿb ibn Asīd heard it and said to his family: "This knocking is your brother who brought misfortune upon his people, and now he has come to bring misfortune and destruction upon us, and to order us to break the covenant between us and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله has fulfilled His obligations to us and has been an excellent neighbor." He descended to him from his chamber and asked him: "Who are you?" He replied: "Huyayy ibn Akhtab. I have brought you the glory of the age." Kaʿb said: "Rather, you have brought me the humiliation of the age." Kaʿb said: "Here is Quraysh, with its leaders and masters, camped at Al-ʿAqīq, along with their allies from Kinanah. And here is Fazārah, with its leaders and masters, camped at Al-Zaghābah. And here are Sulaym and others, camped at the fortress of Banu Dhubyān. Muhammad and His Companions will never escape this gathering. So open the gate and break the covenant between you and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله." Kaʿb said: "I will not open the gate for you. Go back where you came from." Huyayy said: "Nothing prevents you from opening the gate except your grain (حشيشك) in the oven, for fear that I might share it with you. Open up, for you are safe from that." Kaʿb said to him: "May Allah curse you! You have entered upon me through a subtle door (باب دقيق)." Then he said: "Open the gate for him." So they opened it for him. He said: "Woe to you, O Kaʿb! Break the covenant between you and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, and do not reject my opinion, for Muhammad will never escape this gathering. If you miss this opportunity, you will never find another like it."

He said: All the Jewish leaders who were in the fortress gathered, such as Ghazāl ibn Shamūl, Yāsir ibn Qays, Rifāʿah ibn Zayd, and Al-Zubayr ibn Yāṭā. Kaʿb asked them: "What is your opinion?" They replied: "You are our master and the one obeyed among us, and the keeper of our covenant and treaty. If you break it, we break it with you. If you remain, we remain with you. If you leave, we leave with you." Al-Zubayr ibn Yāṭā, who was an old, experienced man whose sight had gone, said: "I have read the Torah (التوراة) that Allah the Almighty revealed in our book (سفرنا), that a Prophet will be sent at the end of time. His emergence will be in Mecca, and His migration will be to this oasis (البحيرة). He rides a bare donkey, wears a coarse cloak (الشملة) with patches (الكسيرات), eats dried grass (يجترني) and dates (التميرات). He is the smiling fighter (الضحوك القتّال), with redness in His eyes, and between His shoulders is the Seal of Prophethood (خاتم النبوّة). He places His sword on His shoulder, caring not whom He meets. His authority will reach the limits of the hoof and the camel's foot (منقطع الخفّ والحافر). If this man is indeed He, then let not these people and their gathering frighten Him. Even if He were to contend with these firm mountains (الجبال الرّواسي), He would overcome them." Huyayy said: "This is not that Prophet. That Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله is from the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), while this one is from the Arabs, from the descendants of Ishmael (peace be upon him). The Children of Israel will never be followers of the descendants of Ishmael (peace be upon him), because Allah has favored them above all people and placed prophethood and kingship among them. Moses (peace be upon him) had covenanted with us: أَلَّا نُؤْمِنَ لِرَسُولٍ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَنا بِقُرْبانٍ تَأْكُلُهُ النَّارُ ** (that we shall not believe in any apostle unless he brings us a sacrifice which the fire consumes). Muhammad has no such sign (آية). He has merely gathered them together and bewitched them, and He seeks to overcome them by that." He continued to turn them away from their opinion until they agreed with him.

He said to them: "Bring out the document (الكتاب) that is between you and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله." They brought it out, and Huyayy ibn Akhtab took it and tore it up, saying: "The matter is settled, so prepare yourselves and get ready for battle." This reached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and it grieved Him greatly, and His Companions were alarmed. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to Saʿd ibn Muʿādh and Usayd ibn Ḥuṣayn—who were from the Aws, and Banu Qurayzah were allies of the Aws—"Go to Banu Qurayzah and see what they have done. If they have broken the covenant, do not inform anyone when you return to Me, but say: 'ʿAḍal and Al-Qārah.'" Saʿd ibn Muʿādh and Usayd ibn Ḥuṣayn came to the gate of the fortress, and Kaʿb looked down upon them from the fortress. He insulted Saʿd and insulted the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. Saʿd said to him: "You are merely a fox in a burrow! Quraysh will surely turn back, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله will surely besiege you, and then He will surely make you submit to humiliation (الصّغر) and subjugation (القماع), and He will surely strike your neck." Then they returned to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and said to Him: "ʿAḍal and Al-Qārah." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Perhaps we ordered them to do that." This was because, during the time of the Messenger of Allah, Quraysh had spies (عيون) who gathered intelligence about Him. ʿAḍal and Al-Qārah were two tribes of Arabs who had entered Islam and then betrayed it (عذرا). So, when someone betrayed, they were cited as an example, and it was said: "ʿAḍal and Al-Qārah."

Huyayy ibn Akhtab returned to Abu Sufyān and Quraysh and informed them of Banu Qurayzah breaking the covenant between them and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. Quraysh rejoiced at this. When it was the middle of the night, Nuʿaym ibn Masʿūd al-Ashjaʿī came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. He had embraced Islam three days before the arrival of Quraysh. He said: "O Messenger of Allah, I have believed in Allah and affirmed You, but I have concealed my faith from the disbelievers. If You command me to come to You myself and support You myself, I will do so. If You command me to sow discord (أخذل) between the Jews and Quraysh, I will do so, so that they do not leave their fortress." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Sow discord between the Jews and Quraysh, for that is more effective for Me." He asked: "Do You permit me to say whatever I wish about You?" He said: "Say whatever occurs to you." So he came to Abu Sufyān and said to him: "Do you know my affection for you, my sincerity, and my desire that Allah grant you victory over your enemy? I have been informed that Muhammad has agreed with the Jews that they should enter your camp and turn against you, and He promised them that if they do this, He will restore to them the portion (جناحهم) that Banu al-Nadir and Qaynuqa lost. Therefore, I do not think you should allow them to enter your camp until you take hostages from them and send them to Mecca, so that you may be safe from their cunning and treachery." Abu Sufyān said to him: "May Allah grant you success and reward you well. A person like you offers the best advice." Neither Abu Sufyān nor any of the Jews knew of Nuʿaym's conversion to Islam.

Then, immediately, he went to Banu Qurayzah and said to Kaʿb: "You know my affection for you. I have been informed that Abu Sufyān said: 'We will bring out these Jews and place them at the forefront against Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. If they are victorious, the credit will be ours, not theirs. If the outcome is against us, they will be the vanguard of the war.' Therefore, I do not think you should allow them to enter your camp until you take ten of their nobles as hostages to be kept in your fortress. If they do not defeat Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, they will not return until they restore your covenant and treaty between Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and you. Because if Quraysh retreats and does not defeat Muhammad, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله will raid you and kill you." They said: "You have done well and given excellent advice. We will not leave our fortress until we take hostages from them to be kept in our fortress."

Quraysh approached. When they looked at the trench, they said: "This is a stratagem (مكيدة) that the Arabs did not know before." It was said to them: "This is from the planning of the Persian (الفارسيّ) who is with Him." ʿAmr ibn ʿAbd Wudd, Hubayrah ibn Wahb, and Ḍirār ibn al-Khaṭṭāb arrived at the trench. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله had lined up His Companions before Him. They shouted at their horses until they leaped the trench to the side of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him). All the Companions of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) retreated behind Him, placing the Messenger of Allah in front of them.

A man from the Emigrants, who was so-and-so (فلان), said to a man next to him, one of his brothers: "Do you not see this devil, ʿAmr? By Allah, no one will escape from his hands. Come, let us hand Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله over to him so he can kill Him, and we will rejoin our people." So Allah the Almighty revealed to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله at that time: قَدْ يَعْلَمُ اللهُ الْمُعَوِّقِينَ مِنْكُمْ ** (Allah certainly knows those who impede [others] among you) up to His saying: وَكانَ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيراً ** (and that is easy for Allah). ʿAmr ibn ʿAbd Wudd planted his spear in the ground and began to circle, reciting rajaz poetry, saying:

I have become hoarse from calling out among your assembly: Is there any challenger?

And I stood, when the brave man was cowardly, in the positions of the contending champion.

Indeed, I have always been rushing toward the battles.

Indeed, courage in a youth and generosity are among the best instincts.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Who will face this dog?" No one answered Him. So the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) leaped toward Him and said: "I am for him, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "O ʿAlī, this is ʿAmr ibn ʿAbd Wudd, a noble knight (فارس نبيل)." He replied: "I am ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: "Come close to Me." So he came close to Him. He placed His turban on him with His own hand, gave him His sword Dhū al-Fiqār (ذا الفقار), and said to him: "Go and fight with this." And He said: "O Allah, protect him from before him, and from behind him, and from his right, and from his left, and from above him, and from beneath him."

The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) passed by, jogging in His walk, saying:

Do not hasten, for the responder to your call has come to you, one who is not incapable.

One possessing intention and insight, and truth saves every victor.

Indeed, I hope to establish over you the wailing woman of funerals.

From a wide blow whose fame will remain after the battles.

ʿAmr said to him: "Who are you?" He replied: "I am ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib, the cousin of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and His son-in-law (ختنه)." He said: "By Allah, your father was a friend and companion of mine, and I hate to kill you. Did your cousin not fear, when He sent you to me, that I would snatch you up with this spear of mine and leave you dangling (شائلاً) between the sky and the earth, neither alive nor dead?" The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to him: "My cousin knows that if you kill me, I will enter Paradise and you will be in the Fire; and if I kill you, you will be in the Fire and I will be in Paradise." ʿAmr said: "Both are for you, O ʿAlī! **تِلْكَ إِذاً قِسْمَةٌ ضِيزى ** (In that case, that would be an unfair division)."

ʿAlī (peace be upon him) said: "Leave this, O ʿAmr. I heard you, while you were clinging to the curtains of the Kaʿbah, saying that no one offers you three choices in battle except that you grant him one of them. I offer you three choices, so grant me one." He said: "Bring them, O ʿAlī." He said: "You bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." He said: "Keep this away from me, and ask the second." He said: "That you return and turn this army away from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. If He is truthful, you will be safe from Him (على به عيناً); and if He is a liar, the wolves (ذؤبان) of the Arabs will suffice you against Him." He said: "In that case, the women of Quraysh would talk about it, and the poets would not recite in their poems that I was cowardly and retreated from the battle, and that I abandoned a people who made me their leader?" The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said to him: "Then the third is that you dismount to fight me, for you are a horseman and I am on foot, so that I may fight you face to face."

So he leaped from his horse and hamstrung it (عرقبه), saying: "This is a choice I never thought any Arab would impose upon me." Then he began and struck the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) with his sword on His head. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) shielded Himself with His shield (الدرقة), which was cut through, and the sword struck His head. ʿAlī (peace be upon him) said to him: "Is it not enough for you that I challenged you, the knight of the Arabs, that you seek help against me with a supporter (بظهير)?" ʿAmr looked behind him, and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) quickly struck him on both his legs, cutting them both off. A cloud of dust (عجاجة) rose between them. The hypocrites (المنافقون) said: "ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (peace be upon him) has been killed." Then the dust cloud cleared, and they looked and saw the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) standing on his chest, holding his beard, intending to slaughter him. Then He took his head and approached the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, with blood flowing from His head due to ʿAmr's blow, and His sword dripping blood. He was saying, with the head in His hand:

I am the son of ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib

Death is better for a youth than flight

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O ʿAlī, you used cunning (ماكَرْتَهُ)?" He replied: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah, war is deception (خديعة)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله sent Al-Zubayr against Hubayrah ibn Wahb, and he struck him on his head with a blow that split his skull (هامته). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded ʿUmar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb to challenge Ḍirār ibn al-Khaṭṭāb to single combat. When Ḍirār came out to him, ʿUmar drew an arrow for him. Ḍirār said to him: "Woe to you, O son of Ṣahhāk! Do you shoot me in single combat? By Allah, if you shoot me, I will not leave any enemy of mine in Mecca except that I will kill him." At that, ʿUmar fled, and Ḍirār passed toward him and struck ʿUmar on his head with the spear (القناة). Then he said: "Remember that, O ʿUmar, for I have sworn not to kill a Qurayshī as long as I am able." ʿUmar remembered this for him after he became ruler (ولّاه).

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله continued fighting them at the trench for fifteen days. Abu Sufyān said to Huyayy ibn Akhtab: "Woe to you, O Jew! Where are your people?" Huyayy ibn Akhtab went to them and said: "Woe to you! Come out! Muhammad has declared war on you, so you are neither with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله nor with Quraysh." Kaʿb said: "We are not leaving until Quraysh gives us ten of their nobles as hostages to be kept in our fortress. If they do not defeat Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, they will not depart until Muhammad restores our covenant and treaty to us. For we do not feel safe that Quraysh might flee and leave us in the heart of our home (عقر دارنا), and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله will raid us, killing our men and taking our women and children captive. If we do not leave, perhaps He will restore our covenant to us." Huyayy ibn Akhtab said to him: "You hope for the impossible! The Arabs have declared war on Muhammad, so you are neither with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله nor with Quraysh." Kaʿb said: "This is from your misfortune (شومك). You are merely a bird that will fly away with Quraysh tomorrow and leave us in the heart of our home, and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله will raid us." He said to him: "Do you have the covenant of Allah and the covenant of Moses upon me that if Quraysh does not defeat Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, I will return with you to your fortress, and whatever befalls you will befall me?" Kaʿb said: "It is what I told you: if Quraysh gives us their nobles as hostages to be kept with us, otherwise we will not leave." Huyayy ibn Akhtab returned to Quraysh and informed them. When he said they were asking for hostages, Abu Sufyān said: "By Allah, this is the first sign of treachery! Nuʿaym ibn Masʿūd spoke the truth. We have no need for the brothers of apes and pigs."

When the matter dragged on for the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and the siege intensified upon them, and they were in severe cold and afflicted by hunger, and they feared the Jews greatly, the hypocrites spoke what Allah the Almighty related about them. None of the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله remained except that he showed hypocrisy (نافق), save for a few. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله had informed His Companions that the Arabs would form a coalition against Him and come to them from above, and the Jews would betray them, and they would fear them from below, and that severe hardship would afflict them, but the final outcome would be His against them. When Quraysh came and the Jews betrayed, the hypocrites said: ما وَعَدَنَا اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ إِلَّا غُرُوراً ** (Allah and His Apostle promised us nothing but delusion). There were some people who had houses on the outskirts of Medina, and they said: "O Messenger of Allah, do You permit us to return to our houses? For they are on the outskirts of Medina and are vulnerable (عورة), and we fear the Jews might raid them." Others said: "Come, let us flee and go into the desert (البادية) and seek refuge with the Bedouins (الأعراب), for what Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله promised us was all falsehood."

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded His Companions to guard Medina at night. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was in charge of the entire army at night, guarding them. If anyone from Quraysh moved, He would confront them. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) would cross the trench and go near Quraysh where He could see them, and He would remain standing alone, praying, all night long. When morning came, He would return to His post. The Mosque of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) there is well-known; those who know it come and pray in it. It is further than an arrow's shot (غلوة النشاب) from the Mosque of the Conquest (مسجد الفتح) to Al-ʿAqīq.

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله saw the distress (الجزع) of His Companions due to the prolonged siege, He ascended to the Mosque of the Conquest—which is the mountain upon which the Mosque of the Conquest stands today—and supplicated to Allah the Almighty and confided in Him concerning what He had promised. Among what He supplicated was: "O Helper of the distressed (يا صريخَ المكروبين), O Responder to the call of the desperate (يا مُجِيبَ دعوةِ المُضطرّينَ), O Remover of the great affliction (يا كاشفَ الكربِ العظيمِ), You are My Master (مَولايَ), My Guardian (وليّي), and the Guardian of My forefathers, the first ones. Remove from us our sorrow (غَمّنا), our anxiety (هَمَّنا), and our affliction (كرْبَنا), and avert from us the evil of these people by Your power (قوّتِكَ), Your might (حولك), and Your capability (قدرتك)." Then Gabriel descended and said: "O Muhammad, Allah the Almighty has heard Your words and answered Your supplication, and He has commanded the Dabūr (the west wind) and the angels to defeat Quraysh and the Confederates." Allah the Almighty sent the Dabūr upon Quraysh, and they were defeated, and their tents were uprooted.

Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended and informed Him of this. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله called Ḥuthayfah ibn al-Yamān رضي الله عنه, who was near Him, but he did not answer Him. Then He called him a second time, but he did not answer Him. Then He called him a third time, and he said: "I am at Your service, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "I call you, but you do not answer Me?" He said: "O Messenger of Allah, may my father and mother be sacrificed for You, it was due to fear, cold, and hunger." He said: "Go among the people and bring Me their news, and do not cause any incident (حدثاً) until you return to Me, for Allah the Almighty has informed Me that He has sent the winds upon Quraysh and defeated them." Ḥuthayfah said: "I went, trembling from the cold. By Allah, no sooner had I crossed the trench than it was as if I were in a bathhouse (الحمّام). I headed toward a large shelter (خبأ), and there was a fire that was dying down and flaring up, and there was a tent in which Abu Sufyān was dangling his testicles over the fire, trembling from the severity of the cold. He was saying: 'O company of Quraysh, if we are fighting the people of the sky, as Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله claims, then we have no power against the people of the sky. But if we are fighting the people of the earth, we can overcome them.' Then he said: 'Let every man among you look at his neighbor, lest there be a spy (عين) of Muhammad among us.'" Ḥuthayfah said: "I quickly spoke up and said to the one on my right: 'Who are you?' He replied: 'I am ʿAmr ibn al-ʿĀṣ.' Then I said to the one on my left: 'Who are you?' He replied: 'I am Muʿāwiyah.' I only rushed to do that so that no one would ask me who I was. Then Abu Sufyān mounted his riding beast, which was hobbled (معقولة). If it were not that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله had said: 'Do not cause any incident until you return to Me,' I would have been able to kill him. Then Abu Sufyān said to Khālid ibn al-Walīd: 'O Abā Sulaymān, you and I must remain to guard the weak among the people.' Then he said: 'Depart! We are departing!'" So they fled, defeated.

When the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) awoke, He said to His Companions: "Do not leave." When the sun rose, they entered Medina, and the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) remained with a small group of people. Ibn ʿArqad al-Kinānī had shot Saʿd ibn Muʿādh with an arrow in the trench, cutting his median vein (اكحله), and he bled profusely. Saʿd grasped his median vein with his hand and then said: "O Allah, if You have left anything of the war against Quraysh, then keep me alive for it, for I find nothing dearer to me than fighting them—a people who fought Allah and His Messenger. But if the war has laid down its burdens (أوزارها) between the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and Quraysh, then make it a martyrdom (شهادة) for me, and do not let me die until my eyes are cooled (تقرّ عيني) concerning Banu Qurayzah." The bleeding stopped, and his hand swelled. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله pitched a tent for him in the Mosque and attended to him Himself.

Then Allah the Almighty revealed: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَةَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ (O you who have faith! Remember Allah’s blessing upon you) the verses up to His saying: إِذْ جاؤُكُمْ مِنْ فَوْقِكُمْ وَمِنْ أَسْفَلَ مِنْكُمْ ** (when they came at you from above you and from below you), meaning Banu Qurayzah, when they betrayed, and the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله feared them. إِذْ زاغَتِ الْأَبْصارُ وَبَلَغَتِ الْقُلُوبُ الْحَناجِرَ ** (when the eyes turned aside and the hearts leaped to the throats) up to His saying: يُرِيدُونَ إِلَّا فِراراً ** (they desire nothing but flight). These are the ones who said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "Do You permit us to return to our homes? For they are on the outskirts of Medina, and we fear the Jews might raid them." So Allah revealed concerning them: إِنَّ بُيُوتَنا عَوْرَةٌ (Indeed, our houses are vulnerable) up to His saying: وَكانَ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيراً ** (and that is easy for Allah). And this verse was revealed concerning the second man who said to ʿAbd al-Raḥmān ibn ʿAwf: "Come, let us hand Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله over to Quraysh, and we will rejoin our people."

(٢١) لَقَدْ كانَ لَكُمْ فِي رَسُولِ اللهِ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ ** (There is certainly a good exemplar for you in the Apostle of Allah) in his actions and his character, such as his steadfastness in war, his enduring of hardships, and other than that. And it was read with the dammah on the hamzah (بضمّ الهمزة). لِمَنْ كانَ يَرْجُوا اللهَ وَالْيَوْمَ الْآخِرَ وَذَكَرَ اللهَ كَثِيراً ** (for him who looks forward to Allah and the Last Day and remembers Allah often) He coupled the abundance of remembrance (كثرة الذكر) with hope (الرجاء), which leads to the adherence to obedience (ملازمة الطاعة), for the one who takes the Apostle as an exemplar (المؤتسى بالرسول) is he who is like that.

(22) وَلَمَّا رَأَ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الْأَحْزابَ قالُوا هذا ما وَعَدَنَا اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ وَصَدَقَ اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ (22) When the faithful saw the confederates, they said, 'This is what Allah and His Apostle promised us, and Allah and His Apostle spoke the truth.' Al-Qummi said: Allah described the faithful ones (المؤمنين) who confirmed what the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله informed them regarding the hardship (الجهد) that would afflict them at the Trench (الخندق). وَما زادَهُمْ ** He said: meaning that affliction (البلاء), hardship (الجهد), and fear (الخوف) إِلَّا إِيماناً وَتَسْلِيماً (except in faith and submission.) It is narrated that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "The matter will intensify with the gathering of the confederates (الأحزاب) against you, but the ultimate outcome (العاقبة) will be yours against them." And He said: "They are marching toward you after nine or ten."

(23) مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ رِجالٌ صَدَقُوا ما عاهَدُوا اللهَ عَلَيْهِ ** (23) Among the faithful are men who fulfilled what they pledged to Allah. They fulfilled their covenant. فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَضى نَحْبَهُ ** So among them are those who fulfilled their vow, (meaning) his vow (نذره). And النّحب (the vow) is borrowed for death because it is like an obligatory vow (نذر لازم) upon the neck (في الرّقبة). وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَنْتَظِرُ ** and among them are those who wait, (meaning) martyrdom (الشهادة). وَما بَدَّلُوا ** and they have not changed [their pledge] (meaning) the covenant (العهد), nor did they alter it. تَبْدِيلاً ** in any way. (meaning) anything of change (التبديل). In this is an allusion (تعريض) to the people of hypocrisy (أهل النفاق) and those with sickness in their hearts (مرض القلب) regarding change (بالتبديل).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: رِجالٌ صَدَقُوا ما عاهَدُوا اللهَ عَلَيْهِ ** (men who fulfilled what they pledged to Allah). He said: "That they would never flee." فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَضى نَحْبَهُ ** (So among them are those who fulfilled their vow), meaning his term (أجله), and that is Hamzah and Ja‘far ibn Abi Talib. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَنْتَظِرُ ** (and among them are those who wait), meaning his term (أجله), referring to ‘Ali (peace be upon him).

In al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in a narration he had with a Jew, he said: "Indeed, I, my uncle Hamzah, my brother Ja‘far, and my cousin ‘Ubaydah had pledged to Allah and His Messenger concerning a matter, and we fulfilled it for Allah the Almighty and His Messenger. My companions preceded me, and I remained after them for what Allah the Almighty willed. So Allah the Almighty revealed concerning us: مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ رِجالٌ صَدَقُوا ** (Among the faithful are men who fulfilled) the Ayah."

In al-Majma‘, narrated from ‘Ali (peace be upon him), he said: "It was revealed concerning us: رِجالٌ صَدَقُوا ** (men who fulfilled). He said: 'By Allah, I am the one who waits (المنتظر), and I have not changed in any way (وما بدّلت تبديلاً).'"

In Sa‘d al-Su‘ud, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: وَكُونُوا مَعَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (and be with the truthful ones). He said: "Be with ‘Ali ibn Abi Talib and the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). Allah the Almighty said: مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ رِجالٌ صَدَقُوا ما عاهَدُوا اللهَ عَلَيْهِ فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَضى نَحْبَهُ ** (Among the faithful are men who fulfilled what they pledged to Allah. So among them are those who fulfilled their vow), and that is Hamzah ibn ‘Abd al-Muttalib. وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَنْتَظِرُ ** (and among them are those who wait), and that is ‘Ali (peace be upon him). Allah says: وَما بَدَّلُوا تَبْدِيلاً (and they have not changed [their pledge] in any way)." In al-Manaqib: Indeed, the companions of al-Husayn (peace be upon him) in Karbala, whenever one of them wished to go out (to battle), he would bid farewell to al-Husayn (peace be upon him) and say: "Peace be upon you, O son of the Messenger of Allah." And He would reply: "And peace be upon you, and we are behind you," and He would recite: فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَضى نَحْبَهُ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَنْتَظِرُ.** (So among them are those who fulfilled their vow, and among them are those who wait.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The believer (المؤمن) is of two types: a believer who was truthful to the covenant of Allah and fulfilled His condition, and that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: رِجالٌ صَدَقُوا ما عاهَدُوا اللهَ عَلَيْهِ ** (men who fulfilled what they pledged to Allah). And that is the one whom the terrors (أهوال) of this world and the terrors of the Hereafter do not afflict. And that is one of those who intercede (يشفع) but for whom intercession is not sought (لا يشفع له). And a believer who is like the stalk of the crop (كحامة الزرع), sometimes bending and sometimes standing upright. That is one of those whom the terrors of this world and the terrors of the Hereafter afflict. And that is one of those for whom intercession is sought (يشفع له) but who does not intercede (ولا يشفع). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah has mentioned you in His Book, saying: مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ رِجالٌ صَدَقُوا ** (Among the faithful are men who fulfilled) the Ayah. Indeed, you have fulfilled what Allah took your covenant upon regarding our Wilayah (ولايتنا), and that you would not replace us with others."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O ‘Ali, whoever loves you and then dies, he has قَضى نَحْبَهُ (fulfilled his vow). And whoever loves you and has not died, he يَنْتَظِرُ (waits). And no sun rises or sets except that it rises upon him with provision (برزق) and faith (وإيمان)." And in one version (وفي نسخة): "light (نور)."

(24) لِيَجْزِيَ اللهُ الصَّادِقِينَ بِصِدْقِهِمْ وَيُعَذِّبَ الْمُنافِقِينَ ** (24) That Allah may reward the truthful for their truthfulness, and punish the hypocrites the changers (المبدّلين) إِنْ شاءَ أَوْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ ** if He wishes, or relent toward them. if they repent, or He grants them success (يوفقهم) for repentance (للتوبة). إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. for whoever repents.

(٢٥) وَرَدَّ اللهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** meaning the Confederates (الأحزاب) بِغَيْظِهِمْ ** being enraged لَمْ يَنالُوا خَيْراً ** not having triumphed وَكَفَى اللهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الْقِتالَ (25) And Allah sent back those who disbelieved in their rage, without having achieved any good, and Allah spared the faithful the fight. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [He spared them] through Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) and his killing of Amr ibn Abd Wudd, and that was the cause of the people's defeat. وَكانَ اللهُ قَوِيًّا ** over bringing about what He wills **عَزِيزاً ** dominant over everything.

وَأَنْزَلَ الَّذِينَ ظاهَرُوهُمْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ مِنْ صَياصِيهِمْ وَقَذَفَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الرُّعْبَ فَرِيقاً تَقْتُلُونَ وَتَأْسِرُونَ فَرِيقاً (٢٦) (26) And He brought down those of the People of the Book who backed them from their strongholds, and cast terror into their hearts. A group you kill, and a group you take captive.

(٢٦) وَأَنْزَلَ الَّذِينَ ظاهَرُوهُمْ ** (26) And He brought down those who backed them They backed the Confederates (الأحزاب). القمّيّ (said): It was revealed concerning Banu Qurayzah. مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ مِنْ صَياصِيهِمْ ** from the People of the Book, from their strongholds meaning: from their fortresses (حصونهم). وَقَذَفَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الرُّعْبَ ** and cast terror into their hearts meaning: fear (الخوف). فَرِيقاً تَقْتُلُونَ وَتَأْسِرُونَ فَرِيقاً. A group you kill, and a group you take captive.**

(٢٧) وَأَوْرَثَكُمْ أَرْضَهُمْ وَدِيارَهُمْ (27) And He made you inherit their land and their homes their farms and their fortresses, وَأَمْوالَهُمْ and their wealth, their money, their livestock, and their furnishings, وَأَرْضاً لَمْ تَطَؤُها وَكانَ اللهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيراً and a land you had not trodden. And Allah is powerful over all things.

Al-Qummi narrates: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله entered Medina while the banner was tied, he intended to perform ghusl (ritual bath) from the dust. Jibrāʾīl called out to him: "Excuse yourself from the fighter (محارب)! By Allah, the angels have not laid down their weapons (لأمتها), so how can you lay down your weapons (لأمتك)? Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commands you not to pray the Afternoon Prayer (al-ʿAsr) except at Banū Qurayẓah, for I shall precede you and shake their fortress for them. We were pursuing the traces of the people, driving them back severely until they reached Ḥamrāʾ al-Asad."

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله went out, and Ḥārithah ibn Nuʿmān met him. He asked him: "What is the news, O Ḥārithah?" He replied: "May my father and mother be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah, this is Diḥyah al-Kalbī calling out among the people: 'Let no one pray the Afternoon Prayer (al-ʿAsr) except at Banū Qurayẓah!'" He said: "That is Jibrāʾīl. Call ʿAlī عليه السلام." So the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام came, and He said to him: "Call out among the people: 'Let no one pray the Afternoon Prayer (al-ʿAsr) except at Banū Qurayẓah!'" So the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام came and called out among them. The people went out and hastened to Banū Qurayẓah.

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله went out, with the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام before Him carrying the Great Banner (الراية العظمى). Ḥuyayy ibn Akhṭab, when Quraysh was defeated, had come and entered the fortress of Banū Qurayẓah. Then the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام came and surrounded their fortress. Kaʿb ibn Asīd looked down upon them from the fortress, insulting them and insulting the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله approached on a donkey. The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام met Him and said: "May my father and mother be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah, do not approach the fortress." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O ʿAlī, perhaps they insulted Me. If they were to see Me, Allah would humiliate them." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله drew near their fortress and said: "O brothers of apes and swine, and worshippers of al-Ṭāghūt (الطاغوت), do you insult Me? Indeed, when We descend upon the courtyard of a people, evil is the morning for those who have been warned." Kaʿb ibn Asīd looked down upon them from the fortress and said: "By Allah, O Abū al-Qāsim, you were not ignorant (جهولاً)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم felt ashamed (استحيا) until His cloak (al-ridāʾ) fell from His back out of shame (حياء) at what he had said.

There were many date palms around the fortress. The Messenger of Allah pointed to them with His hand, and they moved away from Him and scattered into the wilderness (المفازة). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله stationed the army around their fortress and besieged them for three days, during which none of them showed his head. When three days had passed, Ghazāl ibn Shamūl came down to Him and said: "O Muḥammad, will you grant us what you granted our brothers from Banū al-Naḍīr? Spare our blood, and we will leave the land and everything in it for you, and we will conceal nothing from you." He said: "No, unless you submit to My judgment." So he returned, and they remained for some days. The women and children wept to them, and they grieved intensely.

When the siege became severe upon them, they submitted to the judgment of the Messenger of Allah. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded that the men be bound—they were seven hundred—and He commanded that the women be separated. The Aws stood before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "O Messenger of Allah, they are our allies (حلفاؤنا) and our clients (موالينا) among the people; they supported us in going out in all the battles (المواطن). You granted ʿAbd Allāh ibn Ubayy seven hundred armed men (ذراع) and three hundred unarmed men (حاسر) in a single morning, and we are not less worthy than ʿAbd Allāh ibn Ubayy." When they pressed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله repeatedly, He said to them: "Are you not satisfied that the judgment concerning them should be entrusted to a man from among you?" They said: "Yes, and who is he?" He said: "Saʿd ibn Muʿādh." They said: "We have agreed to his judgment."

So they brought him in a litter (محفّة) (1). The Aws gathered around him, saying to him: "O Abū ʿAmr, fear Allah and be good to your allies (حلفائك) and your clients (مواليك). They supported us at Bughāth, al-Ḥadāʾiq, and all the battles (المواطن)." When they pressed him repeatedly, he said: "It is time for Saʿd not to fear the blame of any blamer concerning Allah." The Aws exclaimed: "Woe to our strength (وا قوماه)! By Allah, Banū Qurayẓah is lost forever!" The women and children wept to Saʿd. When they fell silent, Saʿd said to them: "O company of Jews, are you satisfied with my judgment concerning you?" They said: "Yes, we are satisfied with your judgment.
