By Allah, we hoped for your fairness (نصفك), your kindness (معروفك), and your good consideration (حسن نظرك)." He repeated the question to them, and they said: "Yes, O Abū ʿAmr."

He turned to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, showing Him reverence, and said to Him: "What is your view? May my father and mother be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Judge concerning them, O Saʿd, for I have agreed to your judgment concerning them." He said: "I have judged, O Messenger of Allah, that their men shall be killed, their women and children shall be taken captive, and their spoils (غنائمهم) and their wealth shall be divided between the Emigrants (المهاجرين) and the Helpers (الأنصار)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله stood up and said: "You have judged with the judgment of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, from above seven heavens (أرقعة)."

Then the wound of Saʿd ibn Muʿādh burst open, and the blood continued to flow until he passed away. They drove the captives (الأسارى) to Medina. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded that a trench (أخدود) be dug at al-Baqīʿ. When evening came, He commanded that the men be brought out one by one, and their necks were struck. Ḥuyayy ibn Akhṭab said to Kaʿb ibn Asīd: "What do you think He is doing with them?" He replied: "What displeases you. Do you not see that the caller does not reappear, and the one who leaves does not return? You must be patient and steadfast in your religion."

Then Kaʿb ibn Asīd was brought out, his hand bound to his neck. He was handsome and good-looking. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله looked at him, He said to him: "O Kaʿb, did the advice of Ibn Lajjawās, the intelligent scholar (الحبر الذكيّ) who came to you from Syria (الشام), not benefit you?" He replied: "I left wine (الخمر) and leavened bread (الخمير) and came to hardship (البؤس) and dates (التمور) for a Prophet who would be sent, whose place of emergence is Mecca and whose place of migration is in this lake (البحيرة), who would feed me with small pieces of bread (الكسيرات) and small dates (التميرات), and ride a bare donkey, having redness in His eyes and the Seal of Prophethood (خاتم النبوّة) between His shoulders, placing His sword on His shoulder, not caring whom among you He meets, His authority reaching the end of the hoof and the paw (الخفّ والحافر)." He said: "That was indeed the case, O Muḥammad. Were it not that the Jews would reproach me for showing fear (جزعت) at the time of execution, I would have believed in you and affirmed you. But I am upon the religion of the Jews; upon it I live, and upon it I die." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Bring him forward and strike his neck." So it was struck.

Then Ḥuyayy ibn Akhṭab was brought forward. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: "O wicked one (فاسق), how have you seen Allah's doing with you?" He replied: "By Allah, O Muḥammad, I do not blame myself for my enmity toward you. I have stirred every stirrer (قلقلت كلّ مقلقل) and exerted every effort, but whoever Allah forsakes is forsaken." Then, when he was brought forward for execution, he said: "By my life, Ibn Akhṭab did not blame himself, but whoever Allah forsakes is forsaken." He was brought forward and his neck was struck. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله killed them during the two cool times (البردين) (1), morning and evening, over three days. He used to say: "Give them sweet water to drink, feed them good food, and treat their captives well," until He killed all of them.

Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed concerning them to His Messenger: وَأَنْزَلَ الَّذِينَ ظاهَرُوهُمْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ مِنْ صَياصِيهِمْ And He brought down those of the People of the Book who backed them from their strongholds, the Ayah, meaning: from their fortresses (حصُونهم).

(١) Al-Maḥaffah (المحفة)—with a kasrah—is a conveyance for women, like a palanquin (al-hawdaj), except that it is not domed.

(٢٨) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ إِنْ كُنْتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا ** (O Prophet! Say to your wives: 'If you desire the life of the world) Affluence (السعة) and enjoying luxury therein, وَزِينَتَها ** (and its glitter) and its adornments (زخارفها), فَتَعالَيْنَ أُمَتِّعْكُنَ (then come, I will provide for you) I will give you the provision (المتعة), وَأُسَرِّحْكُنَّ سَراحاً جَمِيلاً (٢٨) (and release you with a graceful release.) a divorce (طلاقاً) without harm (ضرار) or innovation (بدعة), willingly (برغبة).

footnote (١). الأبردان : الغداة والعشي. (1). Al-Abaradān: The morning (الغداة) and the evening (العشي).

**وَإِنْ كُنْتُنَّ تُرِدْنَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ وَالدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ فَإِنَّ اللهَ أَعَدَّ لِلْمُحْسِناتِ مِنْكُنَّ أَجْراً عَظِيماً ** (29) But if you desire Allah and His Apostle and the abode of the Hereafter, then Allah has prepared a great reward for the virtuous among you.) [This means] that the world and its adornment are deemed insignificant compared to Him.

Al-Qummi [said]: The reason for its revelation was that when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله returned from the Battle of Khaybar and acquired the treasure of the family of Abu al-Haqiq, his wives said: "Give us what you have acquired." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to them: "I have divided it among the Muslims according to what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded." They became angry about this and said: "Perhaps you think that if you divorce us, we will not find equals (الأكفاء) among our people to marry us." So Allah was protective (أنف) of His Messenger and commanded him to separate from them. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله separated from them in the attic room (مَشْرُبَة) of Umm Ibrahim for twenty-nine days, until they menstruated and became purified. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed this verse, which is the Verse of Choice (آية التخيير). Umm Salamah was the first one who stood up and said: "I have chosen Allah and His Messenger." Then all of them stood up, embraced him, and said the same. Then Allah the Almighty revealed: **تُرْجِي مَنْ تَشاءُ مِنْهُنَّ وَتُؤْوِي إِلَيْكَ مَنْ تَشاءُ ** (You may put off whom you wish of them, and take in whom you wish.) ...the verse.

Al-Sadiq عليه السلام said: "Whoever He takes in (أوى) has married, and whoever He puts off (أرجى) has divorced." So His saying, the Mighty and Majestic, تُرْجِي مَنْ تَشاءُ مِنْهُنَ (You may put off whom you wish of them) is with this verse: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ ** (O Prophet, say to your wives) ...the verse. And it has been placed later than it in the compilation (التأليف).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام in several narrations: Zaynab bint Jahsh said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "Do not be unjust (لا تعدل) while you are a Prophet." He said: "May your hands be covered with dust (تربت يداك). If I am not just, who will be just?" She said: "O Messenger of Allah, I prayed to Allah that my hands be cut off." He said: "No, but rather that they be covered with dust (لتتربان)." Then she said: "If you divorce us, we will find equals (أكفاء) among our people." So the revelation was withheld from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله for twenty-nine nights. He said: So Allah was protective (أنف) of His Messenger, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ ** (O Prophet, say to your wives) ...the two verses. So they chose Allah and His Messenger, and nothing [further] occurred. Had they chosen themselves, they would have been irrevocably separated (لَبِنّ).

And from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Zaynab said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله: "Do not be unjust (لا تعدل) while you are the Messenger of Allah." And Hafsah said: "If you divorce us, we will find our equals (أكفاءنا) from our people." So the revelation was withheld from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله for twenty days. He said: So Allah was protective (أنف) of His Messenger, and He revealed: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ ** (O Prophet, say to your wives) ...the two verses. He said: So they chose Allah and His Messenger. Had they chosen themselves, they would have been irrevocably separated (لَبِنّ). And if they chose Allah and His Messenger, then it is nothing [i.e., it does not constitute a divorce].

And from him عليه السلام: One of the wives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Does Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله think that if He divorces us, we will not find equals (الأكفاء) from our people?" He said: So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, became angry on His behalf from above seven heavens, and commanded Him to give them a choice. When He reached Zaynab bint Jahsh, she stood up, kissed Him, and said: "I choose Allah and His Messenger."

And from him عليه السلام: He was asked about a man who gave his wife a choice, and she chose herself—is she irrevocably separated (بانت)? He said: "No. This was something specific (خاصّة) to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. He was commanded to do this, and He did it. Had they chosen themselves, He would have divorced them. And that is the saying of Allah the Almighty: **قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ إِنْ كُنْتُنَ ** (Say to your wives: 'If you desire') ...the verse."

** يا نِساءَ النَّبِيِّ مَنْ يَأْتِ مِنْكُنَّ بِفاحِشَةٍ مُبَيِّنَةٍ ** (30) O wives of the Prophet, whoever of you commits a gross indecency whose ugliness is manifest, يُضاعَفْ لَهَا الْعَذابُ ضِعْفَيْنِ the punishment shall be doubled for her, twofold, meaning two times the punishment of others, that is, twice its like, because the sin from them is uglier. And it was read (يُضَعَّفْ) with the doubling (تشديد) of the ‘ayn and with the nūn (as نُضَعِّفْ), and the accusative case (نصب) of al-‘adhāb (الْعَذاب). **وَكانَ ذلِكَ عَلَى اللهِ يَسِيراً ** (30) and that is easy for Allah. Their being the wives of the Prophet does not prevent Him from doubling (the punishment). And how could it, when that (status) is the very cause of it.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) who said: The gross indecency (الفاحشة) is rebellion (الخروج) with the sword (بالسيف).

** وَمَنْ يَقْنُتْ مِنْكُنَ ** (And whoever of you is obedient) And whoever persists in obedience ** لِلَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتَعْمَلْ صالِحاً نُؤْتِها أَجْرَها مَرَّتَيْنِ ** (to Allah and His Apostle, and acts righteously, We shall give her her reward twice over) Once for the obedience, and once for their seeking the pleasure of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم through contentment (القناعة), good companionship (حسن المعاشرة), and other such things. And it was read (وقرئ) نعمل and نؤتها with the nūn (النون) in both of them. ** وَأَعْتَدْنا لَها رِزْقاً كَرِيماً ** (and We have prepared for her a noble provision.) in Paradise, in addition to her reward. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said: All of that is in the Hereafter, where the reward is, there is the punishment.

(32) يا نِساءَ النَّبِيِّ لَسْتُنَّ كَأَحَدٍ مِنَ النِّساءِ إِنِ اتَّقَيْتُنَ ** (O wives of the Prophet! You are not like any other women, if you are wary [of Allah]) Allah فَلا تَخْضَعْنَ بِالْقَوْلِ (then do not be soft in speech). It was said: Then do not answer with your speech being submissive and soft, like the speech of women who cause suspicion (المريبات). فَيَطْمَعَ الَّذِي فِي قَلْبِهِ مَرَضٌ ** (lest he in whose heart is a sickness should be tempted) (which is) immorality (فجور). وَقُلْنَ قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً ** (and speak honourable words) Good (حسناً), far from suspicion (الريبة).

(٣٣) وَقَرْنَ فِي بُيُوتِكُنَ [It is derived] from dignity (الوقار) or settling (القرار). And it was recited (وقرئ) with a fathah on the qāf (وقَرْنَ). وَلا تَبَرَّجْنَ تَبَرُّجَ الْجاهِلِيَّةِ الْأُولى (And stay in your houses, and do not flaunt your finery like the flaunting of the former age of ignorance.)

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Ibn Mas'ud, from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in a hadith: Verily, Joshua son of Nun (يوشع ابن نون), the successor (وصيّ) of Moses عليه السلام, lived thirty years after Moses. Safra' daughter of Shu'ayb, the wife of Moses عليه السلام, came out against him and said: "I am more deserving of the authority than you." So he fought her, killed her combatants (مقاتلتها), and treated her captives well. And verily, the daughter of Abu Bakr will come out against Ali in such and such thousands of My Ummah. He will fight her, kill her combatants, and take her captive, treating her captives well. And concerning her, Allah the Almighty revealed: وَقَرْنَ فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ وَلا تَبَرَّجْنَ تَبَرُّجَ الْجاهِلِيَّةِ الْأُولى —meaning Safra' daughter of Shu'ayb.

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq, from his father عليهما السلام, regarding this Ayah. He said: "That is, there will be another age of ignorance (جاهليّة أخرى)." وَأَقِمْنَ الصَّلاةَ وَآتِينَ الزَّكاةَ وَأَطِعْنَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ (And maintain the prayer and give the zakat and obey Allah and His Apostle) in all other matters He has commanded you concerning and forbidden you from. **إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً ** (Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification.)

Al-Qummi said: Then the address to the wives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was interrupted, and He addressed the People of the Household (أهل بيت) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, saying: إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ ** (the Ayah). Then He returned to the wives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, saying: وَاذْكُرْنَ ما يُتْلى ** (And remember what is recited). Then He returned to the progeny of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), saying: إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ ** (the Ayah).

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: This Ayah was revealed concerning the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, Ali ibn Abi Talib, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn عليهم السلام. That was in the house of Umm Salamah, the wife of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم called the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), then He clothed them with a Khaybari cloak (كساء له خيبريّاً) belonging to Him and entered it with them. Then He said: "O Allah, these are My People of the Household (أهل بيتي) concerning whom You promised Me what You promised Me. O Allah, repel the impurity (الرّجس) from them and purify them with a thorough purification (تطهيراً)." Umm Salamah said: "Am I with them, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Rejoice, O Umm Salamah, for you are upon goodness."

And narrated from Zayd ibn Ali ibn al-Husayn عليهم السلام: Verily, the ignorant among the people claim that Allah intended by this Ayah only the wives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and they have lied and sinned. By Allah, had He meant the wives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He would have said: "to repel the impurity from you (feminine plural) and purify you (feminine plural) with a thorough purification." The speech would have been feminine, just as He said: اذْكُرْنَ ما يُتْلى فِي بُيُوتِكُنَ (And remember what is recited in your houses) and وَلا تَبَرَّجْنَ (and do not flaunt your finery) and لَسْتُنَّ كَأَحَدٍ مِنَ النِّساءِ. (You are not like any other women.)

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir عليه السلام: There is nothing further from the minds of men than the interpretation (تفسير) of the Quran. Verily, the beginning of an Ayah may be revealed concerning one matter, its middle concerning another, and its end concerning yet another. Then He said: إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً (Indeed Allah desires to repel all impurity from you, O People of the Household, and purify you with a thorough purification) [meaning] from the birth of the Age of Ignorance (ميلاد الجاهلية).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام regarding this Ayah, He said: "It means the Imams عليهم السلام, and their guardianship (ولايتهم). Whoever enters into it has entered into the house of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم."

And narrated from Him (Imam al-Sadiq), from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, that He said in a hadith: "I enjoin upon you the Book of Allah and My People of the Household (أهل بيتي). Verily, I asked Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, not to separate them until they meet Me at the Pool (الحوض), and He granted Me that. And He said: 'Do not teach them, for they are more knowledgeable than you.' And He said: 'They will never lead you out of a door of guidance (هُدى) nor will they lead you into a door of misguidance (ضلالة).'" He said: Had the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم remained silent and not clarified who His People of the Household were, the family of so-and-so and the family of so-and-so would have claimed it. But Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed in His Book to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ ** (the Ayah). And they were Ali, al-Hasan, al-Husayn, and Fatimah عليهم السلام. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought them under the cloak (الكساء) in the house of Umm Salamah, then He said: "O Allah, verily every Prophet has a family (أهلاً) and a weighty trust (ثقلاً), and these are My People of the Household and My weighty trust." Umm Salamah said: "Am I not from Your family?" He said: "You are towards goodness, but these are My family and My weighty trust." And He said at the end of the hadith: The impurity (الرّجس) is doubt (الشّك), and by Allah, we never doubt our Lord.

And in al-Khisal, concerning the argumentation (احتجاج) of Ali عليه السلام against Abu Bakr, he said: "I adjure you by Allah, is the Ayah of Purification (آية التطهير) from impurity (الرجس) for me, my family, and my children, or for you and your household?" He replied: "Rather, it is for you and your household." He said: "I adjure you by Allah, am I, my family, and my children the subject of the supplication (دعوة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the Day of the Cloak (يوم الكساء): 'O Allah, these are My family, towards You, not towards the Fire,' or are you?" He replied: "Rather, you and your household."

And concerning his argumentation عليه السلام against the people on the Day of the Shura (يوم الشورى), he said: "I adjure you by Allah, is there anyone among you concerning whom Allah revealed the Ayah of Purification to His Messenger: إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ ** (the Ayah), so the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took a Khaybari cloak, gathered me, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn عليهم السلام within it, and then said: 'O Lord, these are My People of the Household, so repel the impurity (الرجس) from them and purify them with a thorough purification (تطهيراً)'—other than me?" They said: "O Allah, no."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: He said to a gathering of the Muhajirun and the Ansar in the Mosque during the days of Uthman's Caliphate: "O people, do you know that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed in His Book: إِنَّما يُرِيدُ اللهُ لِيُذْهِبَ عَنْكُمُ الرِّجْسَ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ وَيُطَهِّرَكُمْ تَطْهِيراً ? So He gathered me, Fatimah, and my two sons, al-Hasan and al-Husayn عليهم السلام, and cast His cloak over us, and said: 'O Allah, these are My People of the Household (أهل بيتي) and My flesh and blood (لحمتي). What pains them pains Me, what grieves them grieves Me, and what distresses them distresses Me. So repel the impurity (الرجس) from them and purify them with a thorough purification (تطهيراً).' Umm Salamah said: 'And me, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'You are, or rather, you are upon goodness. It was revealed specifically concerning Me, My brother, My daughter, My two sons, and nine of the descendants of My son al-Husayn عليهم السلام. No one else is with us.' They all said: 'We bear witness that Umm Salamah narrated that to us, and we asked the Messenger of Allah, and He narrated to us just as Umm Salamah رضي الله عنها narrated to us.'"

And in al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: This Ayah was revealed concerning the Prophet, the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), al-Hasan, al-Husayn, and Fatimah عليهم السلام. When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, took His Prophet, it was the Commander of the Faithful, then al-Hasan, then al-Husayn عليهم السلام. Then the interpretation (تأويل) of this Ayah took effect: وَأُولُوا الْأَرْحامِ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلى بِبَعْضٍ فِي كِتابِ اللهِ (But the blood relatives are closer to one another in the Book of Allah). And it was Ali ibn al-Husayn عليهما السلام. Then it continued among the Imams, the successors (الأوصياء), from his descendants عليهم السلام. Their obedience is obedience to Allah, and their disobedience is disobedience to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic.

I say: The narrations concerning the revelation of this Ayah regarding the status of the Five People of the Cloak (الخمسة أصحاب العباء), from both the Shi'a (الخاصّة) and Sunni (العامّة) paths, are too numerous to count. And al-Majma' has mentioned some of them from the Sunni path; whoever desires them should seek them therein.

(٣٤) وَاذْكُرْنَ ما يُتْلى فِي بُيُوتِكُنَّ مِنْ آياتِ اللهِ وَالْحِكْمَةِ ** (34) And remember what is recited in your houses of the signs of Allah and the wisdom. [This refers to] the Book (الكتاب) that combines the two matters (الامرين). إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ لَطِيفاً خَبِيراً** (Indeed Allah is all-subtle, all-aware.)

(٣٥) إِنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَالْمُسْلِماتِ ** (35) Indeed, the Muslim men and the Muslim women, [These are] those who enter into al-Salam (السلام) and are obedient (المنقادين) to the ruling of Allah, وَالْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ and the believing men and the believing women, [These are] those who affirm (المصدّقين) what must be affirmed.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The Muslim (المسلم) is he from whose hand and tongue the Muslims are safe, and the Believer (المؤمن) is he whose neighbor is safe from his harms (بوائقه). And he has not believed in Me who sleeps satiated while his neighbor is hungry...

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Indeed, al-Iman (الإيمان) is what is firmly established in the hearts, and al-Islam (الإسلام) is what governs marriages (المناكح), inheritances (المواريث), and the protection of blood (حقن الدماء). And al-Iman shares in al-Islam, but al-Islam does not share in al-Iman.

I say: This is supported by the saying of Allah, the Glorious: قالَتِ الْأَعْرابُ آمَنَّا قُلْ لَمْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَلكِنْ قُولُوا أَسْلَمْنا وَلَمَّا يَدْخُلِ الْإِيمانُ فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ (4) The Bedouins say, 'We believe.' Say, 'You have not believed yet; rather say, "We submit," for the faith has not yet entered your hearts.)

وَالْقانِتِينَ وَالْقانِتاتِ ** and the obedient men and the obedient women, [These are] those who persist in obedience (المداومين على الطاعة), وَالصَّادِقِينَ وَالصَّادِقاتِ ** and the truthful men and the truthful women, [These are] those who are truthful in speech (القول) and action (العمل), وَالصَّابِرِينَ وَالصَّابِرَاتِ ** and the patient men and the patient women, [These are those who are patient] in performing acts of obedience (الطاعات) and in refraining from acts of disobedience (المعاصي), وَالْخاشِعِينَ وَالْخاشِعاتِ and the humble men and the humble women, [These are] those who are humble (المتواضعين) before Allah with their hearts (بقلوبهم) and their limbs (وجوارحهم), وَالْمُتَصَدِّقِينَ وَالْمُتَصَدِّقَاتِ ** and the charitable men and the charitable women, [These are those who give charity] from their wealth (أموالهم) seeking the pleasure of Allah (ابتغاء مرضات الله), وَالصَّائِمِينَ وَالصَّائِمَاتِ and the fasting men and the fasting women, [These are those who fast] for Allah with a sincere intention (بنَّيةٍ صادقة), وَالْحافِظِينَ فُرُوجَهُمْ وَالْحافِظاتِ ** and the men who guard their private parts and the women who guard their private parts, [They guard them] from the unlawful (الحرام), وَالذَّاكِرِينَ اللهَ كَثِيراً وَالذَّاكِرَاتِ and the men who remember Allah frequently and the women who remember [Him], [They remember Him] with their hearts (بقلوبهم) and their tongues (وألسنتهم), أَعَدَّ اللهُ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةً Allah has prepared for them forgiveness [for] their sins, وَأَجْراً عَظِيماً and a great reward [for] their obedience (على طاعتهم).

In al-Majma', narrated from Muqatil ibn Hayyan: When Asma bint Umays returned from Abyssinia (الحبشة) with her husband Ja‘far ibn Abi Talib, she entered upon the wives of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and asked: "Has anything of the Quran been revealed concerning us?" They replied: "No." So she went to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, indeed women are in disappointment (خيبة) and loss (خسار)." He asked: "And why is that?" She replied: "Because they are not mentioned favorably (بخير) as men are mentioned." So Allah, the Almighty, revealed this Ayah.

(٣٦) وَما كانَ It is not right ** لِمُؤْمِنٍ وَلا مُؤْمِنَةٍ إِذا قَضَى اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَمْراً أَنْ that it should be And it was read (وقرء) with the yā’ (بالياء) ** لَهُمُ الْخِيَرَةُ مِنْ أَمْرِهِمْ that they should choose anything from their affair (أن يختاروا من أمرهم شيئاً). Rather, it is obligatory upon them (بل يجب عليهم) that they make their choice (أن يجعلوا اختيارهم) subordinate (تبعاً) to the choice of Allah and His Apostle. And al-khīrah (الخيرة) is that which is chosen (ما يتخيّر). A narrative concerning this āyah has already passed in Sūrat al-Qaṣaṣ (سورة القصص). ** وَمَنْ يَعْصِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلالاً مُبِيناً. ** (36) And it is not for a faithful man or a faithful woman, when Allah and His Apostle have decided an affair, that they should have any choice in their affair. And whoever disobeys Allah and His Apostle has certainly strayed into a manifest error.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): That was because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله proposed, on behalf of Zayd ibn Ḥārithah (زيد بن حارثة), for Zaynab bint Jaḥsh al-Asadiyyah (زينب بنت جحش الأسديّة) from Banū Asad ibn Khuzaymah (بني أسد بن خزيمة), and she was the daughter of the Prophet’s صلى الله عليه وآله paternal aunt. So she said: "O Messenger of Allah, wait until I consult my soul (حتّى أوامر نفسي)." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: ** وَما كانَ لِمُؤْمِنٍ وَلا مُؤْمِنَةٍ (And it is not for a faithful man or a faithful woman...) the āyah. So she said: "O Messenger of Allah, my affair is in Your hand." So He married her to him. The narrative (الحديث). And its completion will come shortly.

(٣٧) وَإِذْ تَقُولُ لِلَّذِي أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ بالإسلام وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ بالعتق وهو زيد أبن حارثة أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ زينب وَاتَّقِ اللهَ في أمرها فلا تطلّقها وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللهُ مُبْدِيهِ وهو أنّها ستكون من أزواجه وأنّ زيداً سيطلّقها وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ تعييرهم إيّاك به وَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشاهُ إن كان فيه ما يخشى. (37) And when you said to him whom Allah had blessed and whom you had blessed, ‘Retain your wife, and be wary of Allah,’ and you were concealing in your heart what Allah was going to disclose, and you were fearing the people, and Allah is worthier that you should fear Him.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): That which he concealed in his heart was that Allah, the Glorified, had informed him that she would be among His wives and that Zayd would divorce her. So when Zayd came and said to him, "I want to divorce Zaynab," he said to him: أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ ** (Retain your wife). So, the Glorified One said: "Why did you say: أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ ** (Retain your wife), when I had already informed you that she would be among your wives?"

فَلَمَّا قَضى زَيْدٌ مِنْها وَطَراً (So when Zayd had fulfilled his purpose with her)—a need, such that he grew weary of her and no longer had any need for her, and he divorced her and her waiting period (عدّتها) ended— زَوَّجْناكَها (We married her to you). It was read in the irregular readings (الشواذ): زوّجتكها (We married her to you). In al-Jawami', it is stated that this is the reading of the People of the House (أهل البيت) (peace be upon them). He said: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "I never recited it to my father except in this manner." Until he said: "And ‘Ali (peace be upon him) never recited it to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم except in this manner." He said: And it is narrated that Zaynab used to say to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I boast over you with three things, which none of your wives boasts with: My grandfather and your grandfather are one, Allah married me to you, and the ambassador (السّفير) was Gabriel (جبرئيل)."

لِكَيْ لا يَكُونَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَرَجٌ فِي أَزْواجِ أَدْعِيائِهِمْ إِذا قَضَوْا مِنْهُنَّ وَطَراً علّة للتزويج وَكانَ أَمْرُ اللهِ مَفْعُولاً so that there may be no fault in the believers regarding the wives of their adopted sons, when they have fulfilled their purpose with them. (This is) the reason (علّة) for the marriage. And Allah’s command is bound to be fulfilled.

(٣٨) ما كانَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ مِنْ حَرَجٍ فِيما فَرَضَ اللهُ ** (There is no blame on the Prophet) لَهُ ** (in what Allah has ordained for him) and decreed.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), completing the previous hadith, who said: So He married her to him (Zayd). She remained with Zayd for as long as Allah willed. Then they disputed about something before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله looked at her, and she pleased Him. Zayd then said: "O Messenger of Allah, do you permit me to divorce her, for she has arrogance (كبراً) and she harms me with her tongue." The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said: اتَّقِ اللهَ ** (Be wary of Allah) and أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ ** (Hold on to your wife) and treat her well. Then Zayd divorced her, and her waiting period (عدّتها) ended. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down the marriage of her to His Messenger.

He said: And something other than this has also been narrated concerning it, which We have already transmitted regarding His saying, the Almighty: وَما جَعَلَ أَدْعِياءَكُمْ أَبْناءَكُمْ ** (nor has He made your adopted sons your [real] sons) at the beginning of this surah.

I say: We have already mentioned that narration there.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in the hadith concerning the infallibility (عصمة) of the Prophets, he said: "As for Muhammad, and the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَتُخْفِي فِي نَفْسِكَ مَا اللهُ مُبْدِيهِ وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشاهُ ** (You were concealing in your heart what Allah was going to disclose, and you were fearing the people, while Allah is worthier that you should fear Him.) Allah, the Exalted, had informed His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله of the names of His wives in the abode of this world and the names of His wives in the Hereafter, and that they are the Mothers of the Believers (أمّهات المؤمنين). One of those He named for him was Zaynab bint Jahsh, who at that time was married to Zayd ibn Harithah. So he concealed her name within himself and did not disclose it, lest any of the hypocrites (المنافقين) should say that he spoke of a woman in a man's house, saying that she is one of his wives, the Mothers of the Believers. He feared the saying of the hypocrites. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَتَخْشَى النَّاسَ وَاللهُ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَخْشاهُ (and you were fearing the people, while Allah is worthier that you should fear Him), meaning: within yourself. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, did not undertake the marriage of any of His creation except the marriage of Hawwa (حوّا) to Adam, and Zaynab to the Messenger of Allah by His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: فَلَمَّا قَضى زَيْدٌ مِنْها وَطَراً زَوَّجْناكَها (So when Zayd had accomplished his purpose with her, We wedded her to you), and Fatimah to Ali (peace be upon him)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) in another hadith concerning the infallibility (عصمة) of the Prophets also: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله intended the house of Zayd ibn Harithah ibn Shurahil al-Kalbi for a matter He desired. He saw his wife bathing, so He said to her: "Glory be to Allah Who created you (سُبْحانَ الله الّذِي خَلَقَكِ)." He only intended by that to declare Allah free from the saying of those who claimed that the angels are the daughters of Allah. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَأَصْفاكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِالْبَنِينَ وَاتَّخَذَ مِنَ الْمَلائِكَةِ إِناثاً إِنَّكُمْ لَتَقُولُونَ قَوْلاً عَظِيماً ** (Has your Lord singled you out for sons, and taken daughters for Himself from among the angels? You indeed say a terrible thing!) The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, when He saw her bathing, said: "Glory be to Allah Who created you (سُبْحانَ الله الّذِي خَلَقَكِ), that He should take a son (وَلَداً) who needs this purification and bathing." When Zayd returned to his home, his wife informed him of the Messenger's visit and His saying to her: "Glory be to Allah Who created you (سُبْحانَ الله الّذِي خَلَقَكِ)." Zayd did not know what He intended by that, so he thought He said it because He was impressed by her beauty. So he came to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and said: "O Messenger of Allah, my wife has bad character, and I wish to divorce her." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said to him: أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَاتَّقِ اللهَ ** (Hold on to your wife, and be wary of Allah) — the Ayah. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had already informed Him of the number of His wives and that this woman was one of them. So He concealed that within Himself and did not disclose it to Zayd, fearing that the people might say that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله was telling His freed slave (مولاه) that his wife would become His wife, thereby criticizing Him for it. So Allah revealed: وَإِذْ تَقُولُ لِلَّذِي أَنْعَمَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ ** (And when you said to him whom Allah had blessed) — meaning with Islam — وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ ** — meaning with emancipation (العتق) — أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ ** (Hold on to your wife) — the Ayah. Then Zayd ibn Harithah divorced her, and she completed her waiting period (اعتدّت منه). So Allah, the Exalted, married her to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and sent down a Quranic verse concerning that. So He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَلَمَّا قَضى زَيْدٌ مِنْها وَطَراً ** (So when Zayd had accomplished his purpose with her) — the Ayah. Then He, the Mighty and Majestic, knew that the hypocrites (المنافقين) would criticize Him for marrying her, so He revealed: ما كانَ عَلَى النَّبِيِ ** (There is no blame on the Prophet) صلى الله عليه وآله مِنْ حَرَجٍ فِيما فَرَضَ اللهُ لَهُ سُنَّةَ اللهِ ** (in what Allah has ordained for him—Allah’s precedent) — He established that as a precedent (سُنَّة) — فِي الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلُ (concerning those who passed away before), meaning the Prophets, which is the negation of blame (نفي الحرج) upon them regarding what He permitted for them. وَكانَ أَمْرُ اللهِ قَدَراً مَقْدُوراً ** (and Allah’s command is a decree determined) — a decided decree (قضاء مقضيّاً) and a definitive ruling (حكماً قطعيّاً).

(1) Her waiting period (عدتها): And there was no inclination in his heart towards her, nor any distress from separating from her.

(٣٩) الَّذِينَ يُبَلِّغُونَ رِسالاتِ اللهِ وَيَخْشَوْنَهُ وَلا يَخْشَوْنَ أَحَداً إِلَّا اللهَ وَكَفى بِاللهِ حَسِيباً ** (39) those who deliver the messages of Allah and fear Him, and fear no one except Allah. Allah is sufficient as a reckoner. So, it is proper that one should fear no one except Him. (٤٠) ما كانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبا أَحَدٍ مِنْ رِجالِكُمْ ** (40) Muhammad is not the father of any of your men, in reality (في الحقيقة). Thus, the relationship that exists between a man and his son, such as the prohibition of marriage due to affinity (حرمة المصاهرة) and other matters, is not established between him and him (the adopted son). Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Zayd ibn Harithah. Quraysh said: "Muhammad is reproaching us for some of us claiming others as adopted sons (بِدَعْوَى بَعْضِنَا بَعْضاً), yet he himself adopted Zayd." أقولُ : Its generality (عمومه) is not invalidated by His being the father of al-Qasim, al-Tayyib, al-Tahir, and Ibrahim, because they did not reach the age of men (مبلغ الرجال). And even if they had reached that age, they would have been His men, not their (the people's) men. Likewise, it is not invalidated by His being the father of the Infallible Imams (peace be upon them), because they are His men, not the men of the people, besides the fact that they are not to be compared to the people. In al-Majma': It has been authentically narrated that He صلى الله عليه وآله said concerning al-Hasan: "Indeed, this son of mine is a master (سَيِّد)." And He also said concerning al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them): "These two sons of mine are Imams, whether they stand up or sit down." أقولُ : Meaning, whether they stand up for the Imamate (بالامامة) or refrain from it (قعدا عنها). And He said: "Indeed, every son of a daughter is attributed to his father, except the children of Fatimah, for I am their father." And what indicates that they are the sons of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله has already passed in Surat al-Nisa' and Surat al-An'am. وَلكِنْ رَسُولَ اللهِ ** but the Apostle of Allah And every Messenger is the father of his community (أبو أمّته), not absolutely, but in the sense that He is compassionate (شفيق), a sincere advisor (ناصح) to them, and obedience and reverence (التوقير) are obligatory upon them. And Zayd is one of them, and there is no birth relationship (ولادة) between him and Him that would prohibit marriage due to affinity (المصاهرة) or other matters. وَخاتَمَ النَّبِيِّينَ (and the Seal of the Prophets), meaning the last of them, the One Who sealed them, or Whom they sealed, according to the difference in the two readings (على اختلاف القراءتين). In al-Manaqib, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, He said: "I am the Seal of the Prophets (خاتم الأنبياء), and you, O Ali, are the Seal of the Successors (خاتم الأوصياء)." And the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله sealed a thousand Prophets, and I sealed a thousand Successors (وصيّ), and I was tasked with what they were not tasked with." وَكانَ اللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيماً ** and Allah is knowing of all things. Thus, He knows who is worthy to seal the Prophethood (النبوّة) and what His status (شأنه) ought to be.

(٤١)** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اذْكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثِيراً ** (41) O you who have faith! Remember Allah with frequent remembrance,

[Meaning:] A remembrance that overwhelms the times and encompasses the types of remembrance that are worthy of Him, such as sanctification (التقديس), glorification (التمجيد), declaration of His Oneness (التهليل), and praise (التحميد).

(٤٢)** وَسَبِّحُوهُ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلاً ** (42) and glorify Him morning and evening.

[Meaning:] The beginning of the day and its end, specifically due to their superiority over all other times, because they are witnessed (مشهودين).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "There is nothing except that it has a limit (حدّ) to which it terminates, except for remembrance (الذكر), for it has no limit to which it terminates. Allah ordained the obligatory duties (الفرائض); whoever performs them, that is their limit. And the month of Ramadan; whoever fasts it, that is its limit. And the Hajj; whoever performs it, that is its limit. Except for remembrance (الذكر), for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عز وجل), was not satisfied with a small amount of it and did not set for it a limit to which it terminates."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Our followers (شيعتنا) are those who, when they are alone, remember Allah frequently."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "The glorification (تسبيح) of Fatimah al-Zahra' is part of the frequent remembrance (الذكر الكثير) about which Allah said: اذْكُرُوا اللهَ ذِكْراً كَثِيراً (Remember Allah with frequent remembrance)." And the narrations concerning frequent remembrance (الذكر الكثير) are too numerous to be counted.

(٤٣) ** هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي عَلَيْكُمْ ** with mercy (بالرّحمة) وَمَلائِكَتُهُ ** by seeking forgiveness (باستغفار) for you and by caring for what sets your affairs right (يصلحكم) لِيُخْرِجَكُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ ** from the darknesses (ظلمات) of disbelief (الكفر) and disobedience (المعاصي) to the light (نور) of faith (الإيمان) and obedience (الطاعة) وَكانَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ رَحِيماً ** (43) He it is who blesses you, and His angels [too], that He may bring you out from darkness into light. He is indeed merciful to the faithful. in that He cared for the rectification (صلاح) of their affairs and the elevation (انافة) of their status. And He employed the near-stationed angels (الملائكة المقرّبين) for that purpose.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Whoever sends blessings (صلّى) upon Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad ten times, Allah and His angels send blessings upon him one hundred times. And whoever sends blessings upon Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad one hundred times, Allah and His angels send blessings upon him one thousand times. Do you not hear the saying of Allah: **هُوَ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي عَلَيْكُمْ وَمَلائِكَتُهُ ** and the rest of the Ayah.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: The angels sent blessings upon me and upon Ali عليه السلام for seven years, and that was because no one prayed (يصلّ) during that time except me and him.

(٤٤) تَحِيَّتُهُمْ يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُ سَلامٌ ** (44) Their greeting on the day they meet Him will be, ‘Peace!’ It is said that this is the annexation (إضافة) of the verbal noun (المصدر) to the object (المفعول), meaning that they are greeted on the Day of meeting Him with safety (بالسلامة) from every disliked thing (مكروه) and affliction (آفة).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The meeting (اللقاء) is the Resurrection (البعث). So understand all that is in the Book of Allah concerning His meeting (لقاءه), for He means by that the Resurrection (البعث). Likewise, His saying: يَوْمَ يَلْقَوْنَهُ سَلامٌ ** (on the day they meet Him will be, ‘Peace!’) means that faith (الإيمان) will not depart from their hearts on the Day they are resurrected (يوم يبعثون).

وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ أَجْراً كَرِيماً ** and He has prepared for them a noble reward. This is Paradise (الجنّة).

(٤٥) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ شاهِداً ** over those to whom You were sent, regarding their affirmation (تَصْدِيقِهِمْ) and their denial (تَكْذِيبِهِمْ), and their salvation (نَجَاتِهِمْ) and their misguidance (ضَلَالِهِمْ), وَمُبَشِّراً وَنَذِيراً (O Prophet! Indeed, We have sent you as a witness, and as a bearer of good news and a warner) (٤٦) وَداعِياً إِلَى اللهِ بِإِذْنِهِ ** and by His facilitation (تَيْسِيرِهِ). (and as a caller to Allah by His leave)

In al-Ilal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, that he said in response to a group (نَفَر) of Jews when they asked him why he was named Muhammad (مُحَمَّداً), Ahmad (أَحْمَدَ), Abu al-Qasim (أَبَا الْقَاسِمِ), a bearer of good news (بَشِيراً), وَنَذِيراً وَداعِياً ** (and a warner and a caller): "As for the Caller (الدَّاعِي), I call people to the religion of my Lord, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ). As for the Warner (النَّذِير), I warn with the Fire those who disobey Me. And as for the Bearer of Good News (الْبَشِير), I give good tidings of Paradise to those who obey Me."

وَسِراجاً مُنِيراً ** by Whom light is sought against the darknesses of ignorance (الْجَهَالَة), and from His light are derived the lights of insight (الْبَصَائِر). (and as a luminous lamp.)

(٤٧) ** وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ بِأَنَّ لَهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ فَضْلاً كَبِيراً ** (47) And give the good news to the faithful that there is a great grace for them from Allah. [This grace is] over the rest of the nations, or [it is] over the reward of their deeds.

(٤٨) ** وَلا تُطِعِ الْكافِرِينَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ ** (48) And do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites, [This is] an incitement for him [the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم] to persist in what He is upon regarding opposing them. وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ إيّاك (and disregard their annoyance of you) and your annoyance of them. وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللهِ (and put your trust in Allah), for He will suffice you against them. وَكَفى بِاللهِ وَكِيلاً (and Allah suffices as a trustee), the One to Whom the affair is entrusted in all circumstances.

Al-Qummi [said] that it was revealed in Mecca five years before the Hijrah. He said: "This is evidence of a discrepancy in the arrangement (التأليف)."

(٤٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا نَكَحْتُمُ الْمُؤْمِناتِ ثُمَّ طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَمَسُّوهُنَ ** (49) O you who have faith! When you marry faithful women and then divorce them before you touch them, meaning: you have sexual intercourse with them (تجامعوهنّ). فَما لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ عِدَّةٍ ** then you do not have to reckon any waiting period for them. meaning: days in which they wait (يتربّصن) for themselves, which you reckon (تَعْتَدُّونَها), meaning you complete their number (تستوفون عددها). فَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ وَسَرِّحُوهُنَّ سَراحاً جَمِيلاً So provide for them and release them in a graceful manner. meaning: without harm (ضرر) or withholding a right (منع حقّ).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding a man who divorces his wife before having intercourse (يدخل بها) with her. He (peace be upon him) said: "He owes her half of the dowry (نصف المهر) if he had stipulated something for her. And if He had not stipulated anything for her, then he must provide for her (فليمتّعها) in the manner that women like her (مثلها من النساء) are provided for."

And in al-Faqih and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: فَمَتِّعُوهُنَ (So provide for them), meaning: Treat them (احملوهنّ) with kindness (معروف) according to what you are able to afford, for they return with sadness (كآبة), loneliness (وحشة), great grief (همّ عظيم), and the gloating (شماتة) of their enemies. Indeed, Allah is Generous (كريم); He is shy (يستحي) and loves the people of shyness (أهل الحياء). Verily, the most honorable (أكرمكم) among you are those who are most generous (أشدّكم إكراماً) to their wives (حلائلكم). And the complete discussion regarding this has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah.

(٥٠) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِنَّا أَحْلَلْنا لَكَ أَزْواجَكَ اللَّاتِي آتَيْتَ أُجُورَهُنَ their dowries (مهورهنّ), because the dowry (المهر) is a wage (أجر) for the sexual relation (البضع) وَما مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ مِمَّا أَفاءَ اللهُ عَلَيْكَ through captivity (بالسبي) وَبَناتِ عَمِّكَ وَبَناتِ عَمَّاتِكَ وَبَناتِ خالِكَ وَبَناتِ خالاتِكَ اللَّاتِي هاجَرْنَ مَعَكَ وَامْرَأَةً مُؤْمِنَةً إِنْ وَهَبَتْ نَفْسَها لِلنَّبِيِّ إِنْ أَرادَ النَّبِيُّ أَنْ يَسْتَنْكِحَها خالِصَةً لَكَ مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (50) O Prophet! We have made lawful for you your wives whom you have given their dowries, and those whom your right hand owns out of what Allah has granted you as spoils, and the daughters of your paternal uncles, and the daughters of your paternal aunts, and the daughters of your maternal uncles, and the daughters of your maternal aunts, who migrated with you, and a faithful woman if she offers herself to the Prophet and the Prophet desires to take her in marriage—[this is] exclusively for you, not for the rest of the faithful.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): A woman from the Ansar came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. She entered upon Him while He was in the house of Hafsah. The woman was dressed and had combed her hair. She entered upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, a woman does not propose to a husband, and I am a widow (أيّم) who has had no husband for a long time (دهر) and no child. Do you have any need? If so, I have offered myself to you if you accept me." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم spoke kindly to her and supplicated for her. Then He said: "O sister of the Ansar, may Allah reward you well on behalf of the Messenger of Allah, for your men have supported me and your women have desired me." Hafsah said to her: "How little is your modesty (حياء), how bold you are (أجراكِ), and how eager you are for men (أنهمكِ)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Stop it, O Hafsah, for she is better than you. She desired the Messenger of Allah, yet you blamed and criticized her." Then He said to the woman: "Depart, may Allah have mercy on you. Allah has made Paradise incumbent upon you because of your desire for me and your seeking my love and pleasure. My command will come to you, if Allah the Almighty wills." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed وَامْرَأَةً مُؤْمِنَةً ** (and a faithful woman)—the Ayah. He said: So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made lawful the offering of a woman's self to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and this is not lawful for anyone else.

And in al-Qummi: The reason for its revelation was that a woman from the Ansar came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, having prepared and adorned herself. She said: "O Messenger of Allah, do you have a need for me? I have offered myself to you." 'A'ishah said to her: "May Allah make you ugly (قبّحكِ الله)! How eager you are for men (ما أنهمكِ للرجال)!" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to her: "Silence, O 'A'ishah, for she desired the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم when you all showed indifference (زهدتنّ) toward Him." Then He said: "May Allah have mercy on you, and on you all, O community of the Ansar. Your men support me, and your women desire me. Return, may Allah have mercy on you, for I await the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic." Then Allah the Almighty revealed وَامْرَأَةً مُؤْمِنَةً ** (and a faithful woman)—the Ayah. Thus, the offering (الهبة) is not lawful except for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

And in al-Majma': It is said that when she offered herself to the Prophet, 'A'ishah said: "What is wrong with women that they offer themselves without a dowry (مهر)?" So the Ayah was revealed. 'A'ishah then said: "I do not see Allah the Almighty except that He hastens to fulfill your desire (هواك)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "And if you obey Allah, He will hasten to fulfill your desire (هواك)."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم married fifteen women, and He consummated the marriage with thirteen of them, and He passed away while married to nine. As for the two with whom He did not consummate the marriage, they were 'Amrah and al-Sanah. As for the thirteen with whom He consummated the marriage, the first of them was Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, then Sawdah bint Zam'ah, then Umm Salamah, whose name was Hind bint Abi Umayyah, then Umm 'Abdillah, then 'A'ishah bint Abi Bakr, then Hafsah bint 'Umar, then Zaynab bint Khuzaymah ibn al-Harith (Umm al-Masakin), then Zaynab bint Jahsh, then Umm Habib Ramlah bint Abi Sufyan, then Maymunah bint al-Harith, then Zaynab bint 'Uways, then Juwayriyyah bint al-Harith, then Safiyyah bint Huyayy ibn Akhtab. And the one who offered herself to the Prophet was Khawlah bint Hakim al-Sulami. He also had two concubines (سريّتان) for whom He allocated a share [of His time] along with His wives: Mariyah al-Qibtiyyah and Rayhanah al-Khandaqiyyah. The nine whom He passed away while married to were 'A'ishah, Hafsah, Umm Salamah, Zaynab bint Jahsh, Maymunah bint al-Harith, Umm Habib bint Abi Sufyan, Safiyyah, Juwayriyyah, and Sawdah. The most virtuous of them were Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, then Umm Salamah, then Maymunah. قَدْ عَلِمْنا ما فَرَضْنا عَلَيْهِمْ فِي أَزْواجِهِمْ regarding the conditions (الشرائط) and the restriction (الحصر) to four [wives] وَما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُهُمْ and this clause (الجملة) is parenthetical (اعتراض) لِكَيْلا يَكُونَ عَلَيْكَ حَرَجٌ meaning, the making lawful of them for you was specifically due to meanings (معان) that necessitate expansion (التوسيع) for you وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً regarding what is difficult to guard against (التحرّز عنه) رَحِيماً by granting expansion (التوسعة) in situations where constraint (الحرج) is likely. We certainly know what We have ordained for them concerning their wives and those whom their right hands own, so that there may be no constraint upon you. And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

(٥١) تُرْجِي مَنْ تَشاءُ مِنْهُنَ ** (You may put off [the turn of] whomever of them you wish) You postpone her and do not marry her, or you divorce her. And it was read (وقرئ) without a hamzah (بغير همز). وَتُؤْوِي إِلَيْكَ مَنْ تَشاءُ ** (and take in whomever you wish) And you draw to yourself and keep whomever you wish. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): "Whoever takes in (آوى) has married, and whoever postpones (أرجى) has not married." And in the narration of al-Qummi: "And whoever postpones (أرجى) has divorced, as previously mentioned." وَمَنِ ابْتَغَيْتَ (And whomever you seek)— meaning, you request/seek— مِمَّنْ عَزَلْتَ فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكَ ** (and there is no sin upon you in seeking any of those whom you have set aside.) in any of that. ذلِكَ أَدْنى أَنْ تَقَرَّ أَعْيُنُهُنَّ وَلا يَحْزَنَّ وَيَرْضَيْنَ بِما آتَيْتَهُنَّ كُلُّهُنَ ** (That is likelier to comfort their eyes, and that they may not grieve, and that they may be content with what you give them, all of them.) That delegation [of authority] to Your will is closer to the comfort of their eyes, the lessening of their grief, and the contentment of all of them, because the ruling concerning all of them is equal in this matter. Then, if You equalize [treatment] among them, they will find that to be a favor (تفضّلاً) from You; and if You prefer some of them, they will know that it is by the ruling of Allah, and thus their souls will be reassured. وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ ما فِي قُلُوبِكُمْ وَكانَ اللهُ عَلِيماً حَلِيماً ** (And Allah knows what is in your hearts, and Allah is all-knowing, all-forbearing.) And Allah is عَلِيماً (All-knowing) of what is in the breasts (بذات الصدور), حَلِيماً (All-forbearing), He does not hasten the punishment, so He is worthy of being feared (يتّقي).

(٥٢) ** لا يَحِلُّ لَكَ النِّساءُ مِنْ بَعْدُ وَلا أَنْ تَبَدَّلَ بِهِنَّ مِنْ أَزْواجٍ ** (52) The women are not lawful for you hereafter, nor that you should exchange them for other wives, The word مِنْ (min) is extra (mazīdah) to emphasize comprehensiveness (الاستغراق). وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ حُسْنُهُنَّ إِلَّا ما مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ وَكانَ اللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ رَقِيباً ** even though their beauty should impress you, except those whom your right hand owns. And Allah is watchful over all things. It was said that the meaning is: The women are not lawful for you hereafter, meaning after the categories (الأجناس) mentioned, whose lawfulness (إحلالهنّ) for you was explicitly stated, nor that you should exchange them for wives from other categories (أجناس أخر). And it was said that its meaning is: The women are not lawful for you hereafter, meaning after your wives whom you gave the choice (خيرتهنّ) and who chose Allah and His Messenger, and they are the nine, as a reward (مكافاة) for their choice of Allah and His Messenger.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "What is meant by لا يَحِلُّ لَكَ النِّساءُ ** (The women are not lawful for you) are those whom Allah has forbidden to you in this Ayah: حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهاتُكُمْ وَبَناتُكُمْ وَأَخَواتُكُمْ** (Forbidden to you are your mothers, and your daughters, and your sisters) until the end of it. And if the matter were as they say, then what was not lawful for Him would have been made lawful for you, because any one of you may exchange [wives] whenever he wishes. But the matter is not as they say. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made it lawful for His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله أن ينكح to marry whichever women He wished, except those forbidden in this Ayah in Surat an-Nisa'."

And similar to this is narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in several narrations (روايات). In some of them: "I see you claiming that what was not lawful for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله is lawful for you." And in some of them: "The narrations (أحاديث) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) are contrary to the narrations (أحاديث) of the people."

And al-Qummi [said regarding] لا يَحِلُّ لَكَ النِّساءُ مِنْ بَعْدُ ** (The women are not lawful for you hereafter): [It refers to] what was forbidden to Him in Surat an-Nisa'. And His saying وَلا أَنْ تَبَدَّلَ بِهِنَّ مِنْ أَزْواجٍ ** (nor that you should exchange them for other wives) is connected to the story of Zayd's wife, وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكَ حُسْنُهُنَ (even though their beauty should impress you). Meaning: The wife of a man whom you approach until he divorces her, so that you may marry her, is not lawful for you. And do not commit this act hereafter.

I say: These narrations (الأخبار) are as you see, and likewise what al-Qummi said. May Allah grant us the understanding of them. And it was said that this Ayah is abrogated (منسوخة) by His saying: تُرْجِي مَنْ تَشاءُ مِنْهُنَّ وَتُؤْوِي إِلَيْكَ مَنْ تَشاءُ ** (You may put off whomsoever of them you wish, and you may take in whomsoever you wish). For although it precedes it in recitation (قراءة), it is preceded by it in revelation (نزولاً).

(٥٣) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتَ النَّبِيِّ إِلَّا أَنْ يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ إِلى طَعامٍ ** (53) O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses unless you are permitted for a meal, to which you are invited غَيْرَ ناظِرِينَ إِناهُ ** not waiting for its time (وقته) or its readiness (إدراكه), derived from (إنّي) meaning the food when it is ready (إذا أدرك). وَلكِنْ إِذا دُعِيتُمْ فَادْخُلُوا فَإِذا طَعِمْتُمْ فَانْتَشِرُوا ** disperse (تفرّقوا) and do not linger (تمكثوا) وَلا مُسْتَأْنِسِينَ لِحَدِيثٍ إِنَّ ذلِكُمْ كانَ يُؤْذِي النَّبِيَ ** due to the narrowness of the dwelling for him and his family, and because it occupied him with what did not concern him فَيَسْتَحْيِي مِنْكُمْ ** (of asking you to leave) وَاللهُ لا يَسْتَحْيِي مِنَ الْحَقِ ** so He commands you to leave وَإِذا سَأَلْتُمُوهُنَّ مَتاعاً ** something beneficial (شيئاً ينتفع به). So ask them for the item (المتاع) مِنْ وَراءِ حِجابٍ ** from behind a screen (الستر).

Al-Qummi (reports): When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم married Zaynab bint Jahsh, and He loved her, He held a wedding feast (أولم) and invited His companions. When His companions ate, they liked (يخبّون) to converse in the presence of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, while He loved to be alone with Zaynab. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَدْخُلُوا بُيُوتَ النَّبِيِّ إِلَّا أَنْ يُؤْذَنَ لَكُمْ ** (O you who have faith! Do not enter the Prophet’s houses unless you are permitted) up to His saying: مِنْ وَراءِ حِجابٍ ** (from behind a screen). This was because they used to enter without permission (بلا إذن).

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When Gabriel came to the Prophet, he would sit before Him in the manner of a servant's sitting (قعدة العبد), and he would not enter until he sought His permission (يستأذنه).

ذلِكُمْ أَطْهَرُ لِقُلُوبِكُمْ وَقُلُوبِهِنَ ** That is more purifying for your hearts and their hearts. from satanic thoughts (الخواطر الشيطانيّة).

وَما كانَ لَكُمْ أَنْ تُؤْذُوا رَسُولَ اللهِ ** صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that you should do what He dislikes (1) وَلا أَنْ تَنْكِحُوا أَزْواجَهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ أَبَداً ** after His death (وفاته) or His separation (فراقه). إِنَّ ذلِكُمْ كانَ عِنْدَ اللهِ عَظِيماً ** a grave sin (ذنباً عظيماً).

(٥٤) ** إِنْ تُبْدُوا شَيْئاً ** such as [the desire for] marrying them, expressed on your tongues, أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ ** in your hearts, فَإِنَّ اللهَ كانَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيماً ** He knows that and will recompense you for it. Al-Qummi [said]: The reason for its revelation was that when Allah revealed **النَّبِيُّ أَوْلى بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَأَزْواجُهُ أُمَّهاتُهُمْ ** (The Prophet is closer to the believers than their own souls, and his wives are their mothers.) and Allah forbade the wives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to the Muslims, Talhah became angry and said: "Muhammad forbids his women to us, yet he marries our women! If Allah causes Muhammad to die, we shall certainly run among the anklets of his women just as he ran among the anklets of our women." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed وَما كانَ لَكُمْ أَنْ تُؤْذُوا رَسُولَ اللهِ (the verse).

I say: This ruling includes those [wives] with whom He had not consummated the marriage.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Hasan al-Basri: that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم married a woman from Banu 'Amir ibn Sa'sa'ah called Sanah, and she was one of the most beautiful women of her time. When 'A'ishah and Hafsah looked at her, they said: "This woman will surely overcome us with her beauty in the eyes of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." So they said to her: "Do not let the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم see any eagerness from you." When she entered upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He reached for her with His hand, and she said: "I seek refuge in Allah." So the hand of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم withdrew from her, and He divorced her and sent her back to her family. And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم married a woman from Kindah, the daughter of Abu al-Jawn. When Ibrahim, the son of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the son of Mariyah al-Qibtiyyah, died, she said: "If he were a Prophet, his son would not have died." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent her back to her family before He had consummated the marriage with her. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed away and Abu Bakr governed the people, the 'Amiriyyah woman and the Kindiyyah woman came to him, having been proposed to [for marriage]. So Abu Bakr and 'Umar gathered and said to them both: "Choose: if you wish, the veil (الحجاب), and if you wish, marriage (الباه)." They both chose marriage (الباه) and got married. Then one of the husbands contracted leprosy (جذام) and the other went mad (جنّ). The narrator said: I related this narration to Zurarah and al-Fudayl, and they narrated from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) that He said: "Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has not forbidden anything except that disobedience has occurred regarding it, to the extent that they even married off the wives of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم after Him," and He mentioned these two, the 'Amiriyyah and the Kindiyyah. Then He said: "If you were to ask them about a man who married a woman and divorced her before consummating the marriage, would she be lawful for his son? They would say no. Yet the Messenger of Allah [has] greater sanctity (حرمة) than their fathers." And in al-Manaqib there is a narration: that this ruling also applies to the Successor (الوصيّ).

And in al-Kafi, raised up to them (peace be upon them) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَما كانَ لَكُمْ أَنْ تُؤْذُوا رَسُولَ اللهِ ** (and it is not for you to hurt the Apostle of Allah), They said: It is concerning 'Ali and the Imams (peace be upon them) كَالَّذِينَ آذَوْا مُوسى ** (like those who hurt Moses) (peace be upon him), فَبَرَّأَهُ اللهُ مِمَّا قالُوا. (then Allah cleared him of what they said.)

footnote (1) Meaning: It is not permissible for you to harm the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم by opposing what He commanded regarding His wives, nor regarding anything else.

(٥٥) لا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِنَّ فِي آبائِهِنَّ وَلا أَبْنائِهِنَّ وَلا إِخْوانِهِنَّ وَلا أَبْناءِ إِخْوانِهِنَّ وَلا أَبْناءِ أَخَواتِهِنَ ** (55) There is no sin upon them in [the matter of] their fathers, nor their sons, nor their brothers, nor their brothers’ sons, nor their sisters’ sons, This is an exception for those from whom covering (الاحتجاب) is not obligatory for them. It is narrated: When the verse of the veil (آية الحجاب) was revealed, the fathers, sons, and relatives said: "O Messenger of Allah, should we also speak to them from behind a veil?" So, the following was revealed: وَلا نِسائِهِنَ meaning the believing women (النساء المؤمنات), وَلا ما مَلَكَتْ أَيْمانُهُنَ And the explanation of this has already passed in Surat al-Nur. وَاتَّقِينَ اللهَ ** (and be wary of Allah) regarding what you have been commanded with. إِنَّ اللهَ كانَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيداً (Indeed Allah is witness over all things.) Nothing hidden is concealed from Him.

(٥٦) إِنَّ اللهَ وَمَلائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً. (56) Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet. O you who have faith! Invoke blessings on him and greet him with a [worthy] greeting.

In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about the meaning of the blessing (صلاة) of Allah, the blessing of His angels, and the blessing of the believer. He said: "The blessing (صلاة) of Allah is mercy from Allah, the blessing (صلاة) of the angels is purification (تزكية) from them for him, and the blessing (صلاة) of the believers is supplication (دعاء) from them for him."

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "The blessing (الصلاة) from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is mercy, and from the angels it is purification (تزكية), and from the people it is supplication (دعاء). As for His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً (and greet him with a [worthy] greeting), it means submission (التسليم) regarding what has been reported from him (peace be upon him)." It was asked: "How do we invoke blessings (نصلّي) upon Muhammad and his Household (آله)?" He said: "You say: 'The blessings (صلوات) of Allah, and the blessings (صلوات) of His angels, and His prophets, and His messengers, and all His creation be upon Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد), and peace be upon him and upon them, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings (بركاته).'" It was asked: "What is the reward for the one who invokes blessings upon the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله with these blessings (الصلوات)?" He said: "Emerging from sins, by Allah, as if on the day his mother gave birth to him."

And al-Qummi said: The blessings (صلوات) of Allah upon him are purification (تزكية) for him and praise upon him, and the blessing (صلاة) of the angels is their commendation (مدحهم) of him, and the blessing (صلاة) of the people is their supplication (دعاؤهم) for him, and the affirmation (التصديق) and acknowledgment (الإقرار) of his merit (فضله). And His saying: وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً (and greet him with a [worthy] greeting) means: submit (سلّموا) to him regarding the guardianship (الولاية) and what he brought.

And in al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "Praise him (أثنوا عليه) and submit to him (سلّموا له)."

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in his session with al-Ma'mun, he said: "The obstinate among them already knew that when this Ayah was revealed, it was said: 'O Messenger of Allah, we have known how to greet you (التسليم), so how should we invoke blessings (الصلاة) upon you?'" He said: "You say: 'O Allah, bless Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد), just as You blessed and bestowed grace upon Abraham and the Household of Abraham (آل إبراهيم). Indeed, You are Praiseworthy, Glorious (حميد مجيد).'" [Al-Rida (peace be upon him) then asked:] "O assembly of people, is there any disagreement among you regarding this?" They said: "No." Al-Ma'mun said: "This is something about which there is absolutely no disagreement, and upon it is the consensus (إجماع) of the Ummah." [Al-Ma'mun then asked:] "Do you have anything clearer than this in the Quran regarding the Household (الآل)?" He (peace be upon him) said: "Yes. Inform me about the saying of Allah, the Almighty: يس وَالْقُرْآنِ الْحَكِيمِ إِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ (1 Y. S. 2 By the wise Quran, 3 you are indeed one of the apostles, 4 on a straight path.) "Whom did He mean by His saying: يس (Yā Sīn)?" The scholars said: "يس (Yā Sīn) is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم; no one doubts that." He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Allah granted Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد) a merit (فضلاً) from that, the essence of whose description no one can reach except those who comprehend it. This is because Allah did not send peace (يسلّم) upon anyone except the prophets. So, He, the Blessed and Exalted, said: سَلامٌ عَلى نُوحٍ فِي الْعالَمِينَ (‘Peace be to Noah throughout the nations!’) And He said: سَلامٌ عَلى إِبْراهِيمَ (‘Peace be to Abraham!’) And He said: سَلامٌ عَلى مُوسى وَهارُونَ (‘Peace be to Moses and Aaron!’) But He did not say: 'Peace be upon the Household of Noah (آل نوح),' nor did He say: 'Peace be upon the Household of Abraham (آل إبراهيم),' nor did He say: 'Peace be upon the Household of Moses and Aaron (آل موسى وهرون).' But He said: سَلامٌ عَلى آلْ ياسِينَ (Peace be to the House of Yasin), meaning the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد), the blessings of Allah be upon them." Al-Ma'mun said: "I knew that the source of prophethood (معدن النبوّة) contains the explanation and clarification of this."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) in what he wrote in Shara'i al-Din (شرايع الدين): "And invoking blessings (الصلاة) upon the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله is obligatory (واجبة) in every situation, and upon sneezing, and [when mentioning] the winds (الرياح), and other than that." And in al-Khisal: A similar narration from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

And in al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "And invoke blessings (صلّ) upon the Prophet whenever you mention him, or whenever a mentioner mentions him in your presence, whether in the call to prayer (أذان) or otherwise."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was taken [in death] (قبض), the angels, the Muhajirun (المهاجرون), and the Ansar (الأنصار) invoked blessings (صلّت) upon him, group after group." He said: And the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله say, during his health and safety: 'This Ayah was only revealed concerning the invocation of blessings upon me after Allah takes me [in death]: إِنَّ اللهَ وَمَلائِكَتَهُ يُصَلُّونَ (Indeed Allah and His angels bless the Prophet...) the Ayah.'" And in it (al-Kafi), narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً), he said: "Indeed, Moses supplicated secretly to Allah, the Exalted, and He said to him in His secret supplication, having mentioned Muhammad: 'So invoke blessings (فصلِّ) upon him, O son of 'Imran, for I invoke blessings upon him, and My angels [do so too].'"

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "This Ayah has an outward meaning (ظاهر) and an inward meaning (باطن). The outward meaning (الظاهر) is His saying: صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ (Invoke blessings on him), and the inward meaning (الباطن) is His saying: وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً (and greet him with a [worthy] greeting), meaning: submit (سلّموا) to the one whom He appointed as his successor (وصّاه) and vicegerent (استخلفه) over you, regarding his merit (فضله) and what He entrusted to him, with complete submission (تسليماً)." He said: "And this is among what I informed you that its interpretation (تأويله) is not known except by one whose perception (حسّه) is subtle, whose mind (ذهنه) is pure, and whose discernment (تمييزه) is sound."

(٥٧) ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ ** (57) Indeed, those who abuse Allah and His Apostle (They commit what He and His Apostle dislike, such as disbelief (الكفر) and disobedience (المخالفة)) ** لَعَنَهُمُ اللهُ ** (He has distanced them from His mercy) ** فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ عَذاباً مُهِيناً ** Allah has cursed them in this world and the Hereafter, and He has prepared for them a humiliating punishment. (which humiliates them along with the pain)

القمّيّ said: It was revealed concerning those who usurped the right of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and took the right of Fatimah (peace be upon her) and harmed her. And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Whoever harms her during my life is like one who harms her after my death, and whoever harms her after my death is like one who harms her during my life. And whoever harms her has harmed me, and whoever harms me has harmed Allah." And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ **

In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): He said, while holding his hair: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله narrated to me, while holding his hair, and said: "Whoever harms a single hair of yours has harmed me, and whoever harms me has harmed Allah, and whoever harms Allah, upon him is the curse of Allah."

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله delayed the last evening prayer (العشاء الآخرة) one night for as long as Allah willed. Then 'Umar came and knocked on the door and said: "O Messenger of Allah, the women have slept, the children have slept." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله came out and said: "It is not for you to harm me or to command me. Rather, it is incumbent upon you to listen and obey."

(٥٨) وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْذُونَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ بِغَيْرِ مَا اكْتَسَبُوا ** (58) And those who abuse the faithful, men and women, without their having earned it, without a crime by which they deserved it. فَقَدِ احْتَمَلُوا بُهْتاناً وَإِثْماً مُبِيناً ** they have certainly borne a calumny and a manifest sin. manifest (ظاهراً). Al-Qummi said: It means ‘Ali and Fatimah (peace be upon them both), and it applies to all people. And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "When the Day of Resurrection comes, a caller will cry out: 'Where are those who harmed My friends (أوليائي)?' Then a group of people will stand up whose faces have no flesh upon them. It will be said: 'These are those who harmed the believers, showed enmity towards them (نصبوا لهم), opposed them (وعاندوهم), and treated them harshly (وعنّفوهم) regarding their religion.' Then they will be commanded to be taken to Hell (جهنّم)." And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "People are two types: a believer (مؤمن) and an ignorant person (جاهل). So, do not harm the believer (المؤمن) and do not act ignorantly (تجهل) towards the ignorant person (الجاهل), lest you become like him." And al-Qummi, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "Whoever calumniates (بهتْ) a believing man (مؤمناً) or a believing woman (مؤمنة) will be made to stand in ṭīnat khabāl (طينة خبال) until he retracts what he said." And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), is a narration similar in meaning, and at its end: He was asked: "And what is ṭīnat khabāl (طينة خبال)?" He said: "Pus (صديد) that flows from the private parts (فروج) of the adulteresses (المومسات)."

(٥٩) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لِأَزْواجِكَ وَبَناتِكَ وَنِساءِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ يُدْنِينَ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ جَلَابِيبِهِنَ ** (59) O Prophet! Tell your wives and your daughters and the women of the faithful to draw their outer garments closer to themselves. They should cover their faces and their bodies with their outer wraps (ملاحفهنّ) when they go out for a need. And the particle مِنْ (مِنْ) is for partiality (للتّبعيض), for the woman lets down part of her outer garment (جلبابها) and wraps herself with part of it. ذلِكَ أَدْنى أَنْ يُعْرَفْنَ ** That is likelier to make them recognizable So that they may be distinguished from the slave-women (الإماء) and the singing-girls (القينات). فَلا يُؤْذَيْنَ ** and not be harassed. So that the people of suspicion (أهل الريبة) do not harass them by exposing themselves to them. وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً ** Allah is all-forgiving, for what has passed, رَحِيماً ** all-merciful. to His servants, as He observes their interests, even the minute details (الجزئيات) thereof. Al-Qummi: The reason for its revelation was that the women used to go out to the mosque and pray behind the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. When it was night and they went out for the Maghrib prayer (صلاة المغرب), the last Isha prayer (العشاء الآخرة), and the Ghadah (dawn) prayer (الغداة), the young men would sit for them on their path, thus harassing them and exposing themselves to them. So Allah revealed: يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُ** (O Prophet!) [up to] the end of the Ayah.

(٦٠) لَئِنْ لَمْ يَنْتَهِ الْمُنافِقُونَ وَالَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ (60) If the hypocrites and those in whose hearts is a sickness (a sickness, meaning) doubt (شكّ) وَالْمُرْجِفُونَ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ and those who spread rumors in the city those who spread rumors (يُرْجِفُونَ) of bad news concerning the detachments (سرايا) of the Muslims and the like. Its origin (أصله) is movement (التحريك) from al-rajfah (الرجفة), which is the earthquake (الزلزلة). The false news (الأخبار الكاذبة) was named by this because it is shaky (مُتَزَلْزِلاً), not firm (غير ثابت). لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ بِهِمْ We shall certainly incite you against them We shall certainly command you to fight them and expel them (إجلائهم), or [We shall command] that which compels them to seek expulsion (الجلاء). ثُمَّ لا يُجاوِرُونَكَ فِيها ** then they will not remain your neighbours in it (in it, meaning) in Medina (المدينة). إِلَّا قَلِيلاً ** except for a little while. a short time (زماناً) or a short passage (جوازاً).

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning a group of hypocrites (منافقين) who were in Medina (المدينة), spreading rumors (يُرْجِفُونَ) about the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله when He went out on some of His campaigns (غزواته). They would say: "He has been killed and captured." The Muslims would therefore be distressed (يَغْتَمُّونَ) and complain about this to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. So Allah revealed concerning that: لَئِنْ لَمْ يَنْتَهِ ** (the Ayah). He said: a sickness (مَرَضٌ), meaning doubt (شكّ). لَنُغْرِيَنَّكَ , meaning: We shall certainly command you to expel them (بِإِخْرَاجِهِمْ) from Medina (المدينة).

(٦١) مَلْعُونِينَ أَيْنَما ثُقِفُوا أُخِذُوا وَقُتِّلُوا تَقْتِيلاً (61) accursed wherever they are found, they shall be seized and slaughtered completely. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: The curse became incumbent upon them. Allah says after the curse: أَيْنَما ثُقِفُوا أُخِذُوا وَقُتِّلُوا تَقْتِيلاً (wherever they are found, they shall be seized and slaughtered completely).
