(٦٢) سُنَّةَ اللهِ فِي الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (62) [This is] Allah’s سنت in those who have passed away before, Allah established that (سُنَّة) in the past nations, which is that those who showed hypocrisy toward the Prophets and strove to weaken them through spreading rumors (الإرجاف) and the like, should be killed wherever they were found. وَلَنْ تَجِدَ لِسُنَّةِ اللهِ تَبْدِيلاً ** and you will never find any change in Allah’s سنت. because He does not change it, and no one is able to change it.

(٦٣) يَسْئَلُكَ النَّاسُ عَنِ السَّاعَةِ ** (The people ask you about the Hour.) about the time of its occurrence (قيامها). قُلْ إِنَّما عِلْمُها عِنْدَ اللهِ ** (Say, 'Its knowledge is only with Allah.') He did not inform an angel (ملكاً) nor a prophet (نبيّاً) about it. وَما يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّ السَّاعَةَ تَكُونُ قَرِيباً (What will apprise you? Maybe the Hour is near.) something near (شيئاً قريباً).

(٦٤) إِنَّ اللهَ لَعَنَ الْكافِرِينَ وَأَعَدَّ لَهُمْ سَعِيراً ** (64) Indeed Allah has cursed the faithless and prepared for them a blaze, A fiercely burning fire. (٦٥) خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً لا يَجِدُونَ وَلِيًّا ** (65) abiding in it forever. They will find no protector who protects them وَلا نَصِيراً ** nor any helper. who repels the punishment from them. (٦٦) يَوْمَ تُقَلَّبُ وُجُوهُهُمْ فِي النَّارِ ** (66) The day their faces are turned over in the Fire, [meaning] they are turned away from one direction to another, or from one state to another state يَقُولُونَ يا لَيْتَنا أَطَعْنَا اللهَ وَأَطَعْنَا الرَّسُولَا ** they will say, 'O, would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Apostle!' so that we would not have been afflicted with this punishment. And it was recited (read) similarly to [the reading of] الظُّنُونَا , and likewise the way (السبيل) [of recitation] is the same for السَّبِيلَا**.

(٦٧) وَقالُوا رَبَّنا إِنَّا أَطَعْنا سادَتَنا ** (67) And they will say, 'Our Lord, we obeyed our masters) And it was read ساداتنا (our masters) وَكُبَراءَنا فَأَضَلُّونَا السَّبِيلَا (and our elders, and they led us astray from the way.) (٦٨) رَبَّنا آتِهِمْ ضِعْفَيْنِ مِنَ الْعَذابِ ** (68) Our Lord, give them a double share of the punishment,) like what We gave of it, because they went astray and led us astray وَالْعَنْهُمْ لَعْناً كَبِيراً ** (and curse them with a great curse.') meaning a curse which is the severest and greatest of curses. And it was read كثيراً (many) with the dotted ث (بالمثلثة), meaning numerous in quantity.

Al-Qummi (said): This is an allusion (كناية) to those who usurped the right of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their family). يا لَيْتَنا أَطَعْنَا اللهَ وَأَطَعْنَا الرَّسُولَا (Would that we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Apostle!) meaning regarding the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And the masters (السادة) and the elders (الكبراء) are the first ones who began their oppression and usurpation. فَأَضَلُّونَا السَّبِيلَا ** (and they led us astray from the way.) meaning the path to Paradise. And the Way (السبيل) is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٦٩) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَكُونُوا كَالَّذِينَ آذَوْا مُوسى فَبَرَّأَهُ اللهُ مِمَّا قالُوا ** (69) O you who have faith! Do not be like those who tormented Moses, whereat Allah cleared him of what they alleged. So He manifested his innocence from their allegation. وَكانَ عِنْدَ اللهِ وَجِيهاً ** and he was highly regarded by Allah. possessing nearness (قربة) and high standing (وجاهة).

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Children of Israel used to say that Moses did not possess what men possess. When Moses wished to bathe, he would go to a place where none of the people could see him. One day, he was bathing on the bank of a river, having placed his clothes upon a rock. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded the rock, and it moved away from him (peace be upon him) until the Children of Israel looked at him. They then knew that it was not as they had alleged. So Allah revealed the Ayah.

In al-Majalis, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Verily, the contentment (رضا) of people cannot be possessed, and their tongues cannot be controlled. Did they not attribute to Moses that he was impotent (عنّين) and torment him until فَبَرَّأَهُ اللهُ مِمَّا قالُوا وَكانَ عِنْدَ اللهِ وَجِيهاً. (whereat Allah cleared him of what they alleged; and he was highly regarded by Allah.)"

In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): Moses and Aaron (peace be upon them both) ascended the mountain, and Aaron (peace be upon him) died. The Children of Israel said: "You killed him." So Allah commanded the angels, and they carried him until they passed by the Children of Israel, and the angels spoke of his death until they knew that he had indeed died. Thus, Allah cleared him of that.

And in a marfu' (elevated) narration: Moses (peace be upon him) was modest (حييئاً) and concealed (ستيراً), bathing alone. They said: "He only conceals himself from us because of a defect in his skin, either leprosy (برص) or scrotal swelling (أدرة)." So, he went one time to bathe and placed his garment upon a stone. The stone ran off with his garment, and Moses (peace be upon him) pursued it. The Children of Israel saw him naked, being the most handsome of men in creation. فَبَرَّأَهُ اللهُ مِمَّا قالُوا. (whereat Allah cleared him of what they alleged.)

(٧٠) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَقُولُوا قَوْلاً سَدِيداً (70) O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and speak upright words. (٧١) يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ أَعْمالَكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ (71) He will set right your conduct for you and forgive you your sins.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He said to 'Abbad ibn Kathir al-Sufi al-Basri: "Woe unto you, O 'Abbad! Has it deceived you that your stomach and your private parts are chaste? Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and the Majestic, says in His Book: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَقُولُوا قَوْلاً سَدِيداً يُصْلِحْ لَكُمْ أَعْمالَكُمْ ** Know that Allah will not accept anything from you until you speak a just word (قولاً عدلاً). وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَقَدْ فازَ فَوْزاً عَظِيماً.**" (And whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle has certainly achieved a great success.)

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and the Majestic: وَمَنْ يُطِعِ اللهَ وَرَسُولَهُ (And whoever obeys Allah and His Apostle), [it refers] to the guardianship (ولاية) of 'Ali (peace be upon him) and the Imams (peace be upon them) after him. **فَقَدْ فازَ فَوْزاً عَظِيماً ** Thus was it revealed.

إِنَّا عَرَضْنَا الْأَمانَةَ عَلَى السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَالْجِبالِ فَأَبَيْنَ أَنْ يَحْمِلْنَها وَأَشْفَقْنَ مِنْها وَحَمَلَهَا الْإِنْسانُ إِنَّهُ كانَ ظَلُوماً جَهُولاً. (72) We offered the trust to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to bear it, and were apprehensive of it, but man bore it. Indeed, he is wrongful, ignorant.

I say: What has been said in the interpretation of this verse, in the context of generalization (التعميم), is that the intent of the Trust (الْأَمانَةَ) is the religious obligation (التكليف); and the intent of offering it to them is looking at their readiness (استعدادهنّ); and the intent of their refusal (آبائهنّ) is the natural refusal (الآباء الطبيعي) which is the lack of suitability (اللّياقة) and readiness (الاستعداد); and the intent of man bearing it (حمل الإنسان) is his capability (قابليّته) and readiness (استعداده) for it; and his being wrongful (ظلوماً) and ignorant (جهولاً) is due to the dominance of the power of anger (القوّة الغضبيّة) and lust (الشهويّة) over him. This is a description of the species (للجنس) based on the majority (الأغلب). And everything that has been narrated concerning its interpretation (تأويلها) in the context of specification (التخصيص) returns to this meaning, as becomes clear upon reflection (بالتدبر).

In al-Uyun and al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: The Trust (الْأَمانَةَ) is the Guardianship (الولاية). Whoever claims it without right has indeed committed disbelief (كفر).

I say: By Guardianship (الولاية), he means the command (الامرة) and the Imamate (الإمامة). It is possible that the intent is proximity to Allah.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the Guardianship (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

And in al-Basair, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is the Guardianship (الولاية). فَأَبَيْنَ أَنْ يَحْمِلْنَها ** (but they refused to bear it) out of disbelief (كفراً). وَحَمَلَهَا الْإِنْسانُ ** (but man bore it), and the man (الْإِنْسانُ) is أبو فلان (the father of so-and-so).

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Trust الْأَمانَةَ ** is the Guardianship (الولاية), and the man الْإِنْسانُ ** is أبو الشرّور (the father of evils), the hypocrite (المنافق).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), the summary of which is: Allah offered the souls of the Imams (الأئمّة) to the heavens, the earth, and the mountains, and their light enveloped them. He spoke concerning their virtue what He spoke, then He said: "Their Guardianship (ولايتهم) is a Trust (امانة) with My creation. Which of you will bear it with its burdens and claim it for himself?" So, they refused to claim its station (منزلتها) and to wish for its place (محلّها) due to the greatness of their Lord. When Allah settled Adam (peace be upon him) and his wife in Paradise and told them what He told them, Satan incited them to wish for their station (منزلتهم). So, he looked at them with the eye of envy (الحسد), and they were abandoned until they ate from the wheat tree (شجرة الحنطة). The narration continues until he said: "The Prophets of Allah continued after that to preserve this Trust (الأمانة) and inform their successors (أوصياءهم) and the sincere ones (المخلصين) among their nations about it. They refused to bear it and were apprehensive of claiming it. But the man (الْإِنْسانُ) bore it, who is known as the source of all injustice (ظلم) from him until the Day of Resurrection. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: **إِنَّا عَرَضْنَا الْأَمانَةَ ** (We offered the trust...) the verse."

And al-Qummi said: The Trust الْأَمانَةَ ** is the Imamate (الإمامة), the commanding of good (الامر), and the forbidding of evil (النهي). The proof that the Trust الْأَمانَةَ ** is the Imamate is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic, to the Imams (الأئمّة): إِنَّ اللهَ يَأْمُرُكُمْ أَنْ تُؤَدُّوا الْأَماناتِ إِلى أَهْلِها ** (Indeed Allah commands you to deliver the trusts to their owners), meaning the Imamate. So, the Trust (الامانة) is the Imamate. It was offered عَلَى السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَالْجِبالِ فَأَبَيْنَ أَنْ يَحْمِلْنَها ** (to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to bear it)—meaning, that they should claim it or usurp it from its rightful owners (أهلها). وَأَشْفَقْنَ مِنْها وَحَمَلَهَا الْإِنْسانُ ** (and were apprehensive of it, but man bore it)—meaning the first one (الأول). إِنَّهُ كانَ ظَلُوماً جَهُولاً.** (Indeed, he is wrongful, ignorant.)

I say: And this indicates that the specification of the Trust (الأمانة) to the Guardianship (الولاية) and the Imamate (الإمامة), which ultimately refer to one thing, and the specification of man (الإنسان) to the first one (الأول) in these narrations, does not contradict the validity of intending its generality (عمومها) for every trust (امانة) and religious obligation (تكليف), and the inclusion of every accountable person (مكلّف) under the term 'man' (الإنسان). This is due to what you have learned in the introductions of the book concerning the generalization of meanings (تعميم المعاني) and the intention of realities (ارادة الحقائق). In Nahj al-Balagha, among his recommendations to the Muslims: "Then, the delivery of the Trust (أداء الأمانة). Indeed, he who is not worthy of it has failed. It was offered to the built heavens (السماوات المبنيّة), the spread-out earth (الأرض المدحوّة), and the erected mountains of great height (الجبال ذات الطول المنصوبة). There is nothing longer, wider, higher, or greater than them. Had anything been able to refuse by virtue of length, width, strength, or might, they would have refused. But they were apprehensive of the punishment (العقوبة) and understood what was ignored by one weaker than them, who is man (الْإِنْسانُ). إِنَّهُ كانَ ظَلُوماً جَهُولاً. (Indeed, he is wrongful, ignorant.)"

And in al-Kafi, there is what is close to this. And in al-Awali: When the time for prayer approached, Ali (peace be upon him) would writhe (يتململ), tremble (يتزلزل), and change color (يتلّون). It would be said to him: "What is wrong with you, O Commander of the Faithful?" He would reply: "The time for prayer has come, the time for a Trust (أمانة) which Allah offered عَلَى السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَالْجِبالِ فَأَبَيْنَ أَنْ يَحْمِلْنَها وَأَشْفَقْنَ مِنْها. (to the heavens and the earth and the mountains, but they refused to bear it, and were apprehensive of it.)"

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who sends a message to another man saying to him, "Buy a garment for me." So, he searches for it in the market, and and he has something similar to what he finds for him in the market, so he gives him from what he possesses. He (peace be upon him) said: "He should not approach this, nor should he defile himself. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: **إِنَّا عَرَضْنَا الْأَمانَةَ ** (We offered the trust...) the verse." He (peace be upon him) said: "Even if what he has is better than what he finds for him in the market, he should not give him from what he possesses."

I say: There is no contradiction among these narrations, where the Trust (الأمانة) is sometimes specified as the Guardianship (الولاية) and other times as that which generalizes every trust (امانة) and religious obligation (تكليف). This is due to what you have learned in the introductions of the book concerning the permissibility of generalizing the wording (تعميم اللّفظ) so that it encompasses all probable meanings (المعاني المحتملة) by intending the realities (الحقايق) sometimes, and specifying one by one at other times. Then I say: What is stated in the interpretation (تأويل) of this verse in the context of generalization (التعميم) is that the intent of the Trust (الامانة) is the religious obligation (التكليف) of servitude (العبودية) to Allah in its proper manner, and drawing near (التقرّب) to Allah, the Glorified, through it, as is fitting for every servant according to his readiness for it. The greatest of these is the Divine Vicegerency (الخلافة الالهيّة) for its rightful owners (لأهلها), followed by the submission of those who are not its rightful owners to its rightful owners, and the avoidance of claiming its station for oneself, and then the rest of the religious obligations (التكاليف). The intent of offering it to the heavens, the earth, and the mountains is looking at their readiness for that. Their refusal (إبائهن) is the natural refusal (الإباء الطبيعي), which is the expression of their lack of suitability (عدم اللّياقة) for it. And the intent of man bearing it (حمل الإنسان ايّاها) is his undertaking it without deserving it, out of arrogance (تكبّراً) toward its rightful owners, and despite his failure (تقصيره) to perform it to the best of his ability. And his being wrongful (ظلوماً) and ignorant (جهولاً) is due to the dominance of the power of anger (القوّة الغضبيّة) and lust (الشهويّة) over him. This is a description of the species (للجنس) based on the majority (الأغلب). These are the realities of its universal meanings (حقائق معانيها الكلّية). And everything that has been narrated concerning its interpretation (تأويلها) in the context of specification (التخصيص) returns to these realities (الحقايق), as becomes clear upon reflection. And success (التوفيق) is from Allah.

(٧٣)** لِيُعَذِّبَ اللهُ الْمُنافِقِينَ وَالْمُنافِقاتِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ وَالْمُشْرِكاتِ وَيَتُوبَ اللهُ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ ** (73) so that Allah may punish the hypocritical men and hypocritical women, and the polytheistic men and polytheistic women, and that Allah may relent to the faithful men and faithful women.

This is a justification (تعليل) for the bearing (of the trust, الحمل) in that it is its result. And the mention of repentance (التّوبة) in the promise (الوعد) is an indication (اشعار) that their being "wrongdoing (ظلوماً), ignorant (جهولاً)" in their nature (جبلّتهم) does not free them from slips (فرطات). وَكانَ اللهُ غَفُوراً رَحِيماً (And Allah is All-forgiving, All-merciful) since He relented concerning their slips (فرطاتهم) and rewarded them with success (الفوز) for their obedience (طاعتهم).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever frequently recites Surah al-Ahzab will be on the Day of Resurrection in the neighborhood (جوار) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and His wives." And it is added in Thawab al-A'mal: Then he said: "Surah al-Ahzab exposed (فضحت) the women of Quraysh among the Arabs. And it used to be longer than Surah al-Baqarah, but they reduced it (نقصوها) and distorted it (حرّفوها)."
